validator with default settings\n36\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n37\t        Self::default()\n38\t    }\n39\t\n40\t    /// Create a new syntax validator with strict indentation checking\n41\t    pub }\n48\t\n49\t    /// Create a new lenient syntax validator\n50\t    pub fn lenient() -> Self {\n51\t        Self {\n52\t            strict_indentation: false,\n53\t            check_duplicate_keys: false,\n54\t            validate_block_scalars: false,\n55\t        }\n56\t    }\n57\t\n58\t    /// Validate YAML content from a string\n59\t    ///\n60\t    /// # Arguments\n61\t    /// * `content` - The YAML content to validate\n62\t    ///\n63\t    /// # Returns\n64\t    /// A ValidationResult containing any errors or warnings found\n65\t    pub fn validate(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n66\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n67\t        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n68\t\n69\t        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n70\t        let mut context = ValidationContext::new();\n71\t\n72\t        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n73\t            let line_num_1indexed = line_num + 1;\n74\t\n75\t            // Skip empty lines and comment-only lines for most checks\n76\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n77\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n78\t                continue;\n79\t            }\n80\t\n81\t            // Run each validation pass\n82\t            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_indentation(line, line_num_1indexed, &context) {\n83\t                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n84\t            }\n85\t\n86\t            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_delimiters(line, line_num_1indexed) {\n87\t                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n88\t            }\n89\t\n90\t            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_structure(line, line_num_1indexed, &context) {\n91\t                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n92\t            }\n93\t\n94\t            // Update context for next line\n95\t            context.update_from_line(line, line_num_1indexed);\n96\t        }\n97\t\n98\t        // Final validation checks\n99\t        if let Err(mut final_errors) = self.validate_final_structure(&context) {\n100\t            errors.append(&mut final_errors);\n101\t        }\n102\t\n103\t        ValidationResult {\n104\t            valid: errors.is_empty(),\n105\t            errors,\n106\t            warnings,\n107\t        }\n108\t    }\n109\t\n110\t    /// Validate indentation on a single line\n111\t    fn validate_indentation(\n112\t        &self,\n113\t        line: &str,\n114\t        line_num: usize,\n115\t        context: &ValidationContext,\n116\t    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n117\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n118\t\n119\t        // Check for tabs in strict mode\n120\t        if self.strict_indentation && line.contains('\\t') {\n121\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n122\t                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n123\t                \"tabs are not allowed in YAML (use spaces for indentation)\"\n124\t            ).with_line(line_num));\n125\t        }\n126\t\n127\t        // Check for mixed indentation (tabs and spaces)\n128\t        let leading_bytes = line.as_bytes();\n129\t        let mut has_tabs = false;\n130\t        let mut has_spaces = false;\n131\t\n132\t        for &byte in leading_bytes.iter().take_while(|&&b| b == b' ' || b == b'\\t') {\n133\t            if byte == b'\\t' {\n134\t                has_tabs = true;\n135\t            } else if byte == b' ' {\n136\t                has_spaces = true;\n137\t            }\n138\t        }\n139\t\n140\t        if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n141\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n142\t                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n143\t                \"mixed tabs and spaces in indentation\"\n144\t            ).with_line(line_num));\n145\t        }\n146\t\n147\t        // Check indentation consistency\n148\t        let current_indent = self.count_indentation(line);\n149\t        if let Some(prev_indent) = context.last_indentation {\n150\t            // Check for indentation that's not a multiple of 2 (common convention)\n151\t            if self.strict_indentation && current_indent % 2 != 0 && current_indent > 0 {\n152\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n153\t                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n154\t                    &format!(\"indentation of {} spaces is not a multiple of 2\", current_indent)\n155\t                ).with_line(line_num));\n156\t            }\n157\t\n158\t            // Check for dramatic indentation increases (more than 2 levels at once)\n159\t            if current_indent > prev_indent + 4 {\n160\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n161\t                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n162\t                    &format!(\"indentation increased by {} levels (should increase by at most 2)\",\n163\t                             current_indent - prev_indent)\n164\t                ).with_line(line_num));\n165\t            }\n166\t        }\n167\t\n168\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n169\t            Ok(())\n170\t        } else {\n171\t            Err(errors)\n172\t        }\n173\t    }\n174\t\n175\t    /// Validate delimiters on a single line\n176\t    fn validate_delimiters(\n177\t        &self,\n178\t        line: &str,\n179\t        line_num: usize,\n180\t    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n181\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n182\t\n183\t        // Check for flow mapping/sequence delimiter balance\n184\t        let mut brace_stack = VecDeque::new();\n185\t        let mut bracket_stack = VecDeque::new();\n186\t\n187\t        for (col, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n188\t            match ch {\n189\t                '{' => brace_stack.push_front(('{', col)),\n190\t                '}' => {\n191\t                    if brace_stack.pop_front().is_none() {\n192\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n193\t                            format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n194\t                            \"unmatched closing brace '}'\"\n195\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n196\t                    }\n197\t                }\n198\t                '[' => bracket_stack.push_front(('[', col)),\n199\t                ']' => {\n200\t                    if bracket_stack.pop_front().is_none() {\n201\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n202\t                            format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n203\t                            \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\"\n204\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n205\t                    }\n206\t                }\n207\t                ':' => {\n208\t                    // Check if colon is used as key-value separator\n209\t                    // (followed by space or end of line)\n210\t                    let next_char = line.chars().nth(col + 1);\n211\t                    if next_char.map_or(false, |c| !c.is_whitespace() && c != ':') {\n212\t                        // This might be a value part (like in \"http://\")\n213\t                        // but if it's at the end of non-whitespace, it's suspicious\n214\t                        let remaining = &line[col..];\n215\t                        if remaining.trim_matches(':').is_empty() || remaining.trim().starts_with('#') {\n216\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n217\t                                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n218\t                                \"key-value colon without value\"\n219\t                            ).with_line(line_num));\n220\t                        }\n221\t                    }\n222\t                }\n223\t                _ => {}\n224\t            }\n225\t        }\n226\t\n227\t        // Report unmatched opening delimiters\n228\t        for (delimiter, col) in brace_stack {\n229\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n230\t                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n231\t                &format!(\"unmatched opening brace '{{' at column {}\", col + 1)\n232\t            ).with_line(line_num));\n233\t        }\n234\t\n235\t        for (delimiter, col) in bracket_stack {\n236\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n237\t                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n238\t                &format!(\"unmatched opening bracket '[' at column {}\", col + 1)\n239\t            ).with_line(line_num));\n240\t        }\n241\t\n242\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n243\t            Ok(())\n244\t        } else {\n245\t            Err(errors)\n246\t        }\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    /// Validate structure on a single line\n250\t    fn validate_structure(\n251\t        &self,\n252\t        line: &str,\n253\t        line_num: usize,\n254\t        context: &ValidationContext,\n255\t    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n256\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n257\t\n258\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n259\t\n260\t        // Check for invalid document start marker\n261\t        if trimmed == \"---\" || line.trim_start().starts_with(\"---\") {\n262\t            if !line.trim_start().starts_with(\"--- \") && trimmed != \"---\" {\n263\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n264\t                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n265\t                    \"document start marker '---' must be followed by space or be on its own line\"\n266\t                ).with_line(line_num));\n267\t            }\n268\t        }\n269\t\n270\t        // Check for invalid document end marker\n271\t        if trimmed == \"...\" || line.trim_start().starts_with(\"...\") {\n272\t            if !line.trim_start().starts_with(\"... \") && trimmed != \"...\" {\n273\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n274\t                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n275\t                    \"document end marker '...' must be followed by space or be on its own line\"\n276\t                ).with_line(line_num));\n277\t            }\n278\t        }\n279\t\n280\t        // Check for block scalar indicators\n281\t        if self.validate_block_scalars {\n282\t            if trimmed.starts_with('|') || trimmed.starts_with('>') {\n283\t                let indicator = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n284\t                // Check if indicator is followed by valid modifier or space\n285\t                let rest = &trimmed[1..];\n286\t                if !rest.is_empty() && !rest.starts_with(' ') && !rest.starts_with('-') && !rest.starts_with('+') {\n287\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n288\t                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n289\t                        &format!(\"block scalar indicator '{}' must be followed by space, '+', '-', or end of line\", indicator)\n290\t                    ).with_line(line_num));\n291\t                }\n292\t            }\n293\t        }\n294\t\n295\t        // Check for anchor and alias definitions\n296\t        if trimmed.contains('&') {\n297\t            for (col, _) in line.match_indices('&') {\n298\t                // Check if anchor name is valid\n299\t                let rest = &line[col + 1..];\n300\t                let rest_trimmed = rest.trim();\n301\t\n302\t                // If there's nothing after the &, or only whitespace, it's invalid\n303\t                if rest_trimmed.is_empty() {\n304\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n305\t                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n306\t                        \"anchor '&' must be followed by a name\"\n307\t                    ).with_line(line_num));\n308\t                }\n309\t            }\n310\t        }\n311\t\n312\t        if trimmed.contains('*') {\n313\t            for (col, _) in line.match_indices('*') {\n314\t                let rest = &line[col + 1..];\n315\t                let rest_trimmed = rest.trim();\n316\t\n317\t                // If there's nothing after the *, or only whitespace, it's invalid\n318\t                if rest_trimmed.is_empty() {\n319\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n320\t                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n321\t                        \"alias '*' must be followed by a name\"\n322\t                    ).with_line(line_num));\n323\t                }\n324\t            }\n325\t        }\n326\t\n327\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n328\t            Ok(())\n329\t        } else {\n330\t            Err(errors)\n331\t        }\n332\t    }\n333\t\n334\t    /// Final structure validation after processing all lines\n335\t    fn validate_final_structure(&self, context: &ValidationContext) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n336\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n337\t\n338\t        // Check for unbalanced flow containers across the document\n339\t        if context.open_braces > 0 {\n340\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n341\t                \"document\",\n342\t                &format!(\"unmatched opening brace(s) - {} unclosed\", context.open_braces)\n343\t            ));\n344\t        }\n345\t\n346\t        if context.open_brackets > 0 {\n347\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n348\t                \"document\",\n349\t                &format!(\"unmatched opening bracket(s) - {} unclosed\", context.open_brackets)\n350\t            ));\n351\t        }\n352\t\n353\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n354\t            Ok(())\n355\t        } else {\n356\t            Err(errors)\n357\t        }\n358\t    }\n359\t\n360\t    /// Count the indentation level of a line\n361\t    fn count_indentation(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n362\t        let leading_spaces = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ').count();\n363\t        let leading_tabs = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == '\\t').count();\n364\t\n365\t        if leading_tabs > 0 {\n366\t            // Tabs count as 8 spaces (traditional tab width)\n367\t            leading_spaces + (leading_tabs * 8)\n368\t        } else {\n369\t            leading_spaces\n370\t        }\n371\t    }\n372\t}\n373\t\n374\t/// Validation context maintained during line-by-line processing\n375\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n376\tstruct ValidationContext {\n377\t    /// Last indentation level seen\n378\t    last_indentation: Option<usize>,\n379\t    /// Number of currently open braces\n380\t    open_braces: usize,\n381\t    /// Number of currently open brackets\n382\t    open_brackets: usize,\n383\t    /// Keys seen at current indentation level\n384\t    keys_at_level: Vec<Vec<String>>,\n385\t}\n386\t\n387\timpl ValidationContext {\n388\t    fn new() -> Self {\n389\t        Self::default()\n390\t    }\n391\t\n392\t    fn update_from_line(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize) {\n393\t        let indent = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ' || c == '\\t').count();\n394\t        self.last_indentation = Some(indent);\n395\t\n396\t        // Track brace/bracket balance\n397\t        for ch in line.chars() {\n398\t            match ch {\n399\t                '{' => self.open_braces += 1,\n400\t                '}' => self.open_braces = self.open_braces.saturating_sub(1),\n401\t                '[' => self.open_brackets += 1,\n402\t                ']' => self.open_brackets = self.open_brackets.saturating_sub(1),\n403\t                _ => {}\n404\t            }\n405\t        }\n406\t\n407\t        // Track nested levels for duplicate key checking\n408\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n409\t        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n410\t            let key = trimmed[..colon_pos].trim();\n411\t            if !key.is_empty() && !key.starts_with('#') {\n412\t                // Determine nesting level\n413\t                let level = indent / 2;\n414\t\n415\t                // Ensure we have enough levels\n416\t                while self.keys_at_level.len() <= level {\n417\t                    self.keys_at_level.push(Vec::new());\n418\t                }\n419\t\n420\t                // Check for duplicates\n421\t                if self.keys_at_level[level].contains(&key.to_string()) {\n422\t                    // Would add a warning about duplicate key\n423\t                } else {\n424\t                    self.keys_at_level[level].push(key.to_string());\n425\t                }\n426\t            }\n427\t        }\n428\t    }\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t#[cfg(test)]\n432\tmod tests {\n433\t    use super::*;\n434\t\n435\t    #[test]\n436\t    fn test_validate_empty_content() {\n437\t        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n438\t        let result = validator.validate(\"\");\n439\t        assert!(result.is_valid());\n440\t        assert!(result.errors.is_empty());\n441\t    }\n442\t\n443\t    #[test]\n444\t    fn test_validate_simple_valid_yaml() {\n445\t        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n446\t        let yaml = r#\"\n447\tname: test\n448\tvalue: 42\n449\titems:\n450\t  - one\n451\t  - two\n452\t\"#;\n453\t        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n454\t        assert!(result.is_valid());\n455\t    }\n456\t\n457\t    #[test]\n458\t    fn test_detect_tabs_in_strict_mode() {\n459\t        let validator = SyntaxValidator::strict();\n460\t        let yaml = \"name:\\tvalue\";\n461\t        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n462\t        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n463\t        assert!(result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"tabs\")));\n464\t    }\n465\t\n466\t    #[test]\n467\t    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n468\t        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n469\t        let yaml = \"\\t name: value\";\n470\t        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n471\t        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n472\t        assert!(result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed\")));\n473\t    }\n474\t\n475\t    #[test]\n476\t    fn test_detect_unmatched_brace() {\n477\t        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n478\t        let yaml = \"items: {one: 1, two: 2\";\n479\t        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n480\t        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    #[test]\n484\t    fn test_detect_unmatched_bracket() {\n485\t        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n486\t        let yaml = \"items: [one, two\";\n487\t        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n488\t        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n489\t    }\n490\t\n491\t    #[test]\n492\t    fn test_invalid_block_scalar_indicator() {\n493\t        let validator = SyntaxValidator::strict();\n494\t        let yaml = \"|invalid\";\n495\t        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n496\t        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n497\t    }\n498\t\n499\t    #[test]\n500\t    fn test_anchor_without_name() {\n501\t        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n502\t        let yaml = \"default: &\";\n503\t        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n504\t        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n505\t    }\n506\t}\n507\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e10dfe88-10a1-4c37-bea5-6d2a8b395430","uuid":"b5fa95c8-67ab-4e24-bea0-fb8e6f6aeb97","timestamp":"2026-07-12T23:29:25.791Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/syntax_validator.rs","content":"//! YAML syntax error detection module\n//!\n//! This module provides comprehensive syntax validation for YAML content,\n//! detecting common errors including indentation problems, delimiter issues,\n//! and structure violations.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::types::{ValidationError, ValidationResult};\nuse std::collections::VecDeque;\n\n/// Syntax validator for YAML content\n///\n/// This struct performs line-by-line analysis of YAML content to detect\n/// common syntax errors and structural problems.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SyntaxValidator {\n    /// Whether to use strict indentation checking (disallow tabs)\n    strict_indentation: bool,\n    /// Whether to check for duplicate keys\n    check_duplicate_keys: bool,\n    /// Whether to validate block scalar indicators\n    validate_block_scalars: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for SyntaxValidator {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_indentation: true,\n            check_duplicate_keys: true,\n            validate_block_scalars: true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SyntaxValidator {\n    /// Create a new syntax validator with default settings\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new syntax validator with strict indentation checking\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_indentation: true,\n            check_duplicate_keys: true,\n            validate_block_scalars: true,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new lenient syntax validator\n    pub fn lenient() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_indentation: false,\n            check_duplicate_keys: false,\n            validate_block_scalars: false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content to validate\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ValidationResult containing any errors or warnings found\n    pub fn validate(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n\n        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n        let mut context = ValidationContext::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1indexed = line_num + 1;\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comment-only lines for most checks\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Run each validation pass\n            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_indentation(line, line_num_1indexed, &context) {\n                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n            }\n\n            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_delimiters(line, line_num_1indexed) {\n                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n            }\n\n            if let Err(mut line_errors) = self.validate_structure(line, line_num_1indexed, &context) {\n                errors.append(&mut line_errors);\n            }\n\n            // Update context for next line\n            context.update_from_line(line, line_num_1indexed);\n        }\n\n        // Final validation checks\n        if let Err(mut final_errors) = self.validate_final_structure(&context) {\n            errors.append(&mut final_errors);\n        }\n\n        ValidationResult {\n            valid: errors.is_empty(),\n            errors,\n            warnings,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate indentation on a single line\n    fn validate_indentation(\n        &self,\n        line: &str,\n        line_num: usize,\n        context: &ValidationContext,\n    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Check for tabs in strict mode\n        if self.strict_indentation && line.contains('\\t') {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                \"tabs are not allowed in YAML (use spaces for indentation)\"\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        // Check for mixed indentation (tabs and spaces)\n        let leading_bytes = line.as_bytes();\n        let mut has_tabs = false;\n        let mut has_spaces = false;\n\n        for &byte in leading_bytes.iter().take_while(|&&b| b == b' ' || b == b'\\t') {\n            if byte == b'\\t' {\n                has_tabs = true;\n            } else if byte == b' ' {\n                has_spaces = true;\n            }\n        }\n\n        if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                \"mixed tabs and spaces in indentation\"\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        // Check indentation consistency\n        let current_indent = self.count_indentation(line);\n        if let Some(prev_indent) = context.last_indentation {\n            // Check for indentation that's not a multiple of 2 (common convention)\n            if self.strict_indentation && current_indent % 2 != 0 && current_indent > 0 {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                    &format!(\"indentation of {} spaces is not a multiple of 2\", current_indent)\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            }\n\n            // Check for dramatic indentation increases (more than 2 levels at once)\n            if current_indent > prev_indent + 4 {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                    &format!(\"indentation increased by {} levels (should increase by at most 2)\",\n                             current_indent - prev_indent)\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            }\n        }\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate delimiters on a single line\n    fn validate_delimiters(\n        &self,\n        line: &str,\n        line_num: usize,\n    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Check for flow mapping/sequence delimiter balance\n        let mut brace_stack = VecDeque::new();\n        let mut bracket_stack = VecDeque::new();\n\n        for (col, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n            match ch {\n                '{' => brace_stack.push_front(('{', col)),\n                '}' => {\n                    if brace_stack.pop_front().is_none() {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                            \"unmatched closing brace '}'\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n                '[' => bracket_stack.push_front(('[', col)),\n                ']' => {\n                    if bracket_stack.pop_front().is_none() {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                            \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n                ':' => {\n                    // Check if colon is used as key-value separator\n                    // (followed by space or end of line)\n                    let next_char = line.chars().nth(col + 1);\n                    if next_char.map_or(false, |c| !c.is_whitespace() && c != ':') {\n                        // This might be a value part (like in \"http://\")\n                        // but if it's at the end of non-whitespace, it's suspicious\n                        let remaining = &line[col..];\n                        if remaining.trim_matches(':').is_empty() || remaining.trim().starts_with('#') {\n                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                                \"key-value colon without value\"\n                            ).with_line(line_num));\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Report unmatched opening delimiters\n        for (delimiter, col) in brace_stack {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                &format!(\"unmatched opening brace '{{' at column {}\", col + 1)\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        for (delimiter, col) in bracket_stack {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                &format!(\"unmatched opening bracket '[' at column {}\", col + 1)\n            ).with_line(line_num));\n        }\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate structure on a single line\n    fn validate_structure(\n        &self,\n        line: &str,\n        line_num: usize,\n        context: &ValidationContext,\n    ) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Check for invalid document start marker\n        if trimmed == \"---\" || line.trim_start().starts_with(\"---\") {\n            if !line.trim_start().starts_with(\"--- \") && trimmed != \"---\" {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                    \"document start marker '---' must be followed by space or be on its own line\"\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for invalid document end marker\n        if trimmed == \"...\" || line.trim_start().starts_with(\"...\") {\n            if !line.trim_start().starts_with(\"... \") && trimmed != \"...\" {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                    \"document end marker '...' must be followed by space or be on its own line\"\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for block scalar indicators\n        if self.validate_block_scalars {\n            if trimmed.starts_with('|') || trimmed.starts_with('>') {\n                let indicator = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n                // Check if indicator is followed by valid modifier or space\n                let rest = &trimmed[1..];\n                if !rest.is_empty() && !rest.starts_with(' ') && !rest.starts_with('-') && !rest.starts_with('+') {\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                        &format!(\"block scalar indicator '{}' must be followed by space, '+', '-', or end of line\", indicator)\n                    ).with_line(line_num));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for anchor and alias definitions\n        if trimmed.contains('&') {\n            for (col, _) in line.match_indices('&') {\n                // Check if anchor name is valid\n                let rest = &line[col + 1..];\n                let rest_trimmed = rest.trim();\n\n                // If there's nothing after the &, or only whitespace, it's invalid\n                if rest_trimmed.is_empty() {\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                        \"anchor '&' must be followed by a name\"\n                    ).with_line(line_num));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if trimmed.contains('*') {\n            for (col, _) in line.match_indices('*') {\n                let rest = &line[col + 1..];\n                let rest_trimmed = rest.trim();\n\n                // If there's nothing after the *, or only whitespace, it's invalid\n                if rest_trimmed.is_empty() {\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line {}\", line_num),\n                        \"alias '*' must be followed by a name\"\n                    ).with_line(line_num));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Final structure validation after processing all lines\n    fn validate_final_structure(&self, context: &ValidationContext) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Check for unbalanced flow containers across the document\n        if context.open_braces > 0 {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"document\",\n                &format!(\"unmatched opening brace(s) - {} unclosed\", context.open_braces)\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if context.open_brackets > 0 {\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"document\",\n                &format!(\"unmatched opening bracket(s) - {} unclosed\", context.open_brackets)\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Count the indentation level of a line\n    fn count_indentation(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n        let leading_spaces = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ').count();\n        let leading_tabs = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == '\\t').count();\n\n        if leading_tabs > 0 {\n            // Tabs count as 8 spaces (traditional tab width)\n            leading_spaces + (leading_tabs * 8)\n        } else {\n            leading_spaces\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Validation context maintained during line-by-line processing\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct ValidationContext {\n    /// Last indentation level seen\n    last_indentation: Option<usize>,\n    /// Number of currently open braces\n    open_braces: usize,\n    /// Number of currently open brackets\n    open_brackets: usize,\n    /// Keys seen at current indentation level\n    keys_at_level: Vec<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationContext {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    fn update_from_line(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize) {\n        let indent = line.chars().take_while(|&c| c == ' ' || c == '\\t').count();\n        self.last_indentation = Some(indent);\n\n        // Track brace/bracket balance\n        for ch in line.chars() {\n            match ch {\n                '{' => self.open_braces += 1,\n                '}' => self.open_braces = self.open_braces.saturating_sub(1),\n                '[' => self.open_brackets += 1,\n                ']' => self.open_brackets = self.open_brackets.saturating_sub(1),\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Track nested levels for duplicate key checking\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n            let key = trimmed[..colon_pos].trim();\n            if !key.is_empty() && !key.starts_with('#') {\n                // Determine nesting level\n                let level = indent / 2;\n\n                // Ensure we have enough levels\n                while self.keys_at_level.len() <= level {\n                    self.keys_at_level.push(Vec::new());\n                }\n\n                // Check for duplicates\n                if self.keys_at_level[level].contains(&key.to_string()) {\n                    // Would add a warning about duplicate key\n                } else {\n                    self.keys_at_level[level].push(key.to_string());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_empty_content() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n        let result = validator.validate(\"\");\n        assert!(result.is_valid());\n        assert!(result.errors.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_simple_valid_yaml() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nname: test\nvalue: 42\nitems:\n  - one\n  - two\n\"#;\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_valid());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_tabs_in_strict_mode() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::strict();\n        let yaml = \"name:\\tvalue\";\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n        assert!(result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"tabs\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n        let yaml = \"\\t name: value\";\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n        assert!(result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_unmatched_brace() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n        let yaml = \"items: {one: 1, two: 2\";\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_unmatched_bracket() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n        let yaml = \"items: [one, two\";\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_invalid_block_scalar_indicator() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::strict();\n        let yaml = \"|invalid\";\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_anchor_without_name() {\n        let validator = SyntaxValidator::new();\n        let yaml = \"default: &\";\n        let result = validator.validate(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":507,"startLine":1,"totalLines":507}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e011843c935b4e99b3032d45","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n2\t//!\n3\t//! ParseResult::success(serde_yaml::Value::Null)\n    }\n\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n        let validator {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c0d41237a9624239bf4cf705","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Error types for YAML parsing\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the error types used throughout the YAML parser.\n4\t//!\n5\t//! # Error Handling Philosophy\n6\t//!\n7\t//! The `ParseError` type follows a structured approach to error handling that emphasizes:\n8\t//!\n9\t//! 1. **Clear categorization** - Each error falls into a distinct category (syntax, I/O, validation, etc.)\n10\t//! 2. **Rich context** - Errors carry location information, context messages, and code snippets\n11\t//! 3. **Composability** - Errors propagate cleanly through the call stack using Rust's `?` operator\n12\t//! 4. **User-friendly output** - Multiple formatting options for different use cases (logging, UI, debugging)\n13\t//!\n14\t//! ## When to Use Each Variant\n15\t//!\n16\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Io` vs Other Variants\n17\t//!\n18\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`] for **file system and I/O operation failures**:\n19\t//! - File not found (`std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound`)\n20\t//! - Permission denied (`std::io::ErrorKind::PermissionDenied`)\n21\t//! - Read/write failures (`std::io::ErrorKind::Other`)\n22\t//! - Network I/O errors (if applicable)\n23\t//!\n24\t//! **Do not use** `Io` for:\n25\t//! - YAML syntax errors → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n26\t//! - Type mismatches → use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n27\t//! - Constraint violations → use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`]\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ```ignore\n30\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Io for file system errors\n31\t//! let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → Io\n32\t//!\n33\t//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use Io for parsing errors\n34\t//! // let error = ParseError::io(\"invalid YAML structure\");  // Wrong! Use Syntax instead\n35\t//! ```\n36\t//!\n37\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8` vs Other Variants\n38\t//!\n39\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`] specifically for **encoding errors**:\n40\t//! - Invalid UTF-8 byte sequences in input\n41\t//! - Failed UTF-8 validation when converting bytes to strings\n42\t//!\n43\t//! **Do not use** `InvalidUtf8` for:\n44\t//! - I/O errors during file reading → use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n45\t//! - General parsing failures → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n46\t//!\n47\t//! ```ignore\n48\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use InvalidUtf8 for encoding errors\n49\t//! let string = String::from_utf8(bytes)?;  // FromUtf8Error → InvalidUtf8\n50\t//!\n51\t//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use InvalidUtf8 for file reading errors\n52\t//! // let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8);  // Wrong! Use Io instead\n53\t//! ```\n54\t//!\n55\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof` vs `ParseErrorKind::Syntax`\n56\t//!\n57\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof`] when **input ends prematurely**:\n58\t//! - Incomplete YAML documents (missing closing brackets, braces, quotes)\n59\t//! - Truncated files or streams\n60\t//! - Multi-document YAML streams that end mid-document\n61\t//!\n62\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`] for **general YAML syntax violations**:\n63\t//! - Invalid indentation\n64\t//! - Invalid escape sequences\n65\t//! - Malformed scalars or mappings\n66\t//! - Any YAML grammar violation that's not specifically EOF-related\n67\t//!\n68\t//! ```ignore\n69\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use UnexpectedEof for incomplete input\n70\t//! if input.ends_with(\"key: \") {\n71\t//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n72\t//! }\n73\t//!\n74\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Syntax for general YAML errors\n75\t//! if !is_valid_indentation(line) {\n76\t//!     return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid indentation\"));\n77\t//! }\n78\t//! ```\n79\t//!\n80\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n81\t//!\n82\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`] when **a value has the wrong Rust/YAML type**:\n83\t//! - Expecting an integer but finding a string\n84\t//! - Expecting a sequence but finding a scalar\n85\t//! - Expecting a boolean but finding a number\n86\t//!\n87\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **semantic constraint violations**:\n88\t//! - Value out of allowed range (e.g., port number > 65535)\n89\t//! - String doesn't match required pattern (e.g., invalid email format)\n90\t//! - Array length constraints violated\n91\t//! - Business logic or schema validation failures\n92\t//!\n93\t//! The key distinction: `TypeMismatch` is about **type**, `Validation` is about **value**.\n94\t//!\n95\t//! ```ignore\n96\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use TypeMismatch for type errors\n97\t//! let port = value[\"port\"].as_i64()\n98\t//!     .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\"))?;\n99\t//!\n100\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for value constraints\n101\t//! if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n102\t//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n103\t//! }\n104\t//! ```\n105\t//!\n106\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n107\t//!\n108\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey`] specifically for **duplicate key errors**:\n109\t//! - YAML mappings with repeated keys (YAML 1.2 spec violation)\n110\t//! - Configuration files with conflicting entries\n111\t//!\n112\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **general validation failures** including:\n113\t//! - Missing required fields (not duplicated, but absent)\n114\t//! - Inter-field constraint violations (e.g., \"start_time > end_time\")\n115\t//! - Schema validation failures beyond type/duplicate checking\n116\t//!\n117\t//! ```ignore\n118\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use DuplicateKey for duplicate keys\n119\t//! if keys.contains(&key) {\n120\t//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey(key)));\n121\t//! }\n122\t//!\n123\t//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for missing required fields\n124\t//! if value[\"name\"].is_null() {\n125\t//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: 'name'\"));\n126\t//! }\n127\t//! ```\n128\t//!\n129\t//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Other` (Catch-all) vs Specific Variants\n130\t//!\n131\t//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Other`] **only as a last resort** for errors that don't fit other categories:\n132\t//! - Unclassifiable serde_yaml errors\n133\t//! - Errors from external libraries that don't map cleanly\n134\t//! - Temporary error cases during refactoring\n135\t//!\n136\t//! **Prefer specific variants** whenever possible:\n137\t//! - Unknown anchors → [`ParseErrorKind::UnknownAnchor`]\n138\t//! - Syntax errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n139\t//! - I/O errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n140\t//! - Type errors → [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n141\t//!\n142\t//! If you find yourself using `Other` frequently, consider adding a new specific variant.\n143\t//!\n144\t//! ```ignore\n145\t//! // ✅ Prefer specific variants\n146\t//! return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\"));\n147\t//!\n148\t//! // ⚠️ Use Other only as a catch-all\n149\t//! return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Other(\"unclassified error\".to_string())));\n150\t//! ```\n151\t//!\n152\t//! # Error Propagation Strategy\n153\t//!\n154\t//! The `ParseError` type is designed to work seamlessly with Rust's error propagation\n155\t//! mechanisms. It implements `From` for common error types, enabling the use of the `?`\n156\t//! operator for automatic error conversion.\n157\t//!\n158\t//! ## Basic Propagation with `?`\n159\t//!\n160\t//! ```ignore\n161\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n162\t//!\n163\t//! fn parse_config(path: &Path) -> Result<Config> {\n164\t//!     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → ParseError\n165\t//!     parse_yaml(&content)\n166\t//! }\n167\t//! ```\n168\t//!\n169\t//! ## Adding Context with `.context()`\n170\t//!\n171\t//! Use the builder-style `.context()` method to add contextual information:\n172\t//!\n173\t//! ```ignore\n174\t//! fn parse_database_config(value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n175\t//!     let port = value[\"port\"]\n176\t//!         .as_i64()\n177\t//!         .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"null\"))\n178\t//!         .context(\"while parsing database configuration\")?;\n179\t//!\n180\t//!     Ok(DatabaseConfig { port })\n181\t//! }\n182\t//! ```\n183\t//!\n184\t//! ## Converting from Other Error Types\n185\t//!\n186\t//! `ParseError` implements `From` for:\n187\t//! - `std::io::Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n188\t//! - `serde_yaml::Error` → Appropriate [`ParseErrorKind`] based on error classification\n189\t//! - `std::str::Utf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n190\t//! - `std::string::FromUtf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n191\t//!\n192\t//! ## Custom Error Creation\n193\t//!\n194\t//! For domain-specific errors, create them directly:\n195\t//!\n196\t//! ```ignore\n197\t//! fn validate_port(port: i64) -> Result<u16> {\n198\t//!     if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n199\t//!         return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n200\t//!     }\n201\t//!     Ok(port as u16)\n202\t//! }\n203\t//! ```\n204\t//!\n205\t//! # Examples\n206\t//!\n207\t//! ## Basic Error Creation\n208\t//!\n209\t//! Creating errors using convenience constructors:\n210\t//!\n211\t//! ```\n212\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n213\t//!\n214\t//! // Create a syntax error\n215\t//! let syntax_err = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML indentation\");\n216\t//! assert!(syntax_err.is_syntax());\n217\t//!\n218\t//! // Create a validation error\n219\t//! let validation_err = ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\");\n220\t//! assert!(validation_err.is_validation());\n221\t//!\n222\t//! // Create a type mismatch error\n223\t//! let type_err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n224\t//! assert!(type_err.is_type_mismatch());\n225\t//! assert_eq!(type_err.summary(), \"<unknown>: type mismatch at 'port': expected integer, got string\");\n226\t//! ```\n227\t//!\n228\t//! ## Error Propagation with `?`\n229\t//!\n230\t//! Using the `?` operator to automatically convert errors:\n231\t//!\n232\t//! ```\n233\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n234\t//! use std::fs;\n235\t//!\n236\t//! fn read_config(path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n237\t//!     // io::Error is automatically converted to ParseError via From impl\n238\t//!     let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n239\t//!     Ok(content)\n240\t//! }\n241\t//!\n242\t//! // This function demonstrates automatic error conversion\n243\t//! fn parse_config_size(path: &str) -> Result<usize> {\n244\t//!     let content = read_config(path)?;  // ParseError propagates via ?\n245\t//!     Ok(content.len())\n246\t//! }\n247\t//!\n248\t//! // Example: successful read\n249\t//! assert!(parse_config_size(\"/dev/null\").is_ok());\n250\t//! ```\n251\t//!\n252\t//! ## Custom Error Handling with Builder Pattern\n253\t//!\n254\t//! Using builder methods to add context and location information:\n255\t//!\n256\t//! ```\n257\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n258\t//!\n259\t//! fn validate_service_config(yaml_content: &str) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n260\t//!     // Simulating an error during validation\n261\t//!     if yaml_content.contains(\"port: abc\") {\n262\t//!         let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"service.port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n263\t//!             .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\")\n264\t//!             .with_line(5)\n265\t//!             .with_column(10)\n266\t//!             .with_context(\"while validating service configuration\")\n267\t//!             .with_snippet(\"services:\\n  - name: web\\n    port: abc\");\n268\t//!\n269\t//!         return Err(error);\n270\t//!     }\n271\t//!     Ok(())\n272\t//! }\n273\t//!\n274\t//! // Create an error to verify the builder pattern\n275\t//! let error = ParseError::validation(\"invalid port\")\n276\t//!     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n277\t//!     .with_line(10)\n278\t//!     .with_column(5)\n279\t//!     .with_context(\"while parsing configuration\");\n280\t//!\n281\t//! assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n282\t//! assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n283\t//! assert_eq!(error.column, Some(5));\n284\t//! assert_eq!(error.context, \"while parsing configuration\");\n285\t//! ```\n286\t//!\n287\t//! ## Error Display and Formatting\n288\t//!\n289\t//! Different ways to format and display errors:\n290\t//!\n291\t//! ```\n292\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n293\t//!\n294\t//! // Create an error with full context\n295\t//! let error = ParseError::syntax(\"unexpected colon\")\n296\t//!     .with_path(\"config/database.yaml\")\n297\t//!     .with_line(15)\n298\t//!     .with_column(8)\n299\t//!     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\")\n300\t//!     .with_snippet(\"database:\\n  host: localhost\\n  port: 5432:\\n    invalid\");\n301\t//!\n302\t//! // Get a single-line summary (good for logging)\n303\t//! let summary = error.summary();\n304\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15\"));\n305\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n306\t//! assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n307\t//!\n308\t//! // Get a detailed multi-line report (good for user display)\n309\t//! let detailed = error.detailed_report();\n310\t//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"error:\"));\n311\t//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n312\t//!\n313\t//! // Get a structured representation (good for debugging)\n314\t//! let structured = error.format_structured();\n315\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n316\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15:8\"));\n317\t//! assert!(structured.contains(\"line: Some(15)\"));\n318\t//!\n319\t//! // Display implementation provides user-friendly output\n320\t//! let display_string = format!(\"{}\", error);\n321\t//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"config/database.yaml\"));\n322\t//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n323\t//! ```\n324\t//!\n325\t//! ## Error Conversion from Standard Types\n326\t//!\n327\t//! Automatic conversion from standard library errors:\n328\t//!\n329\t//! ```\n330\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n331\t//! use std::io;\n332\t//!\n333\t//! // io::Error converts to ParseError::Io\n334\t//! let io_err = io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::NotFound, \"file not found\");\n335\t//! let parse_err: ParseError = io_err.into();\n336\t//! assert!(parse_err.is_io());\n337\t//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"I/O error\"));\n338\t//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"file not found\"));\n339\t//!\n340\t//! // String::from_utf8 Error converts to ParseError::InvalidUtf8\n341\t//! let invalid_bytes = b\"\\xff\\xfe\\xfd\";\n342\t//! let utf8_err = String::from_utf8(invalid_bytes.to_vec()).unwrap_err();\n343\t//! let parse_err: ParseError = utf8_err.into();\n344\t//! assert!(matches!(parse_err.kind, armor::parsers::yaml::ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8));\n345\t//! ```\n346\t//!\n347\t//! ## Working with Error Types\n348\t//!\n349\t//! Checking error types and handling specific cases:\n350\t//!\n351\t//! ```\n352\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n353\t//!\n354\t//! let errors = vec![\n355\t//!     ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\"),\n356\t//!     ParseError::io(\"file not found\"),\n357\t//!     ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\"),\n358\t//!     ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"string\", \"integer\"),\n359\t//! ];\n360\t//!\n361\t//! // Check specific error types\n362\t//! assert!(errors[0].is_syntax());\n363\t//! assert!(errors[1].is_io());\n364\t//! assert!(errors[2].is_validation());\n365\t//! assert!(errors[3].is_type_mismatch());\n366\t//!\n367\t//! // Filter errors by type\n368\t//! let syntax_errors: Vec<_> = errors.iter()\n369\t//!     .filter(|e| e.is_syntax())\n370\t//!     .collect();\n371\t//! assert_eq!(syntax_errors.len(), 1);\n372\t//! ```\n373\t\n374\tuse std::fmt;\n375\t\n376\t/// Main error type for YAML parsing operations\n377\t#[derive(Clone)]\n378\tpub struct ParseError {\n379\t    /// The kind of error that occurred\n380\t    pub kind: ParseErrorKind,\n381\t    /// The line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n382\t    pub line: Option<usize>,\n383\t    /// The column number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n384\t    pub column: Option<usize>,\n385\t    /// The file or source path where the error occurred\n386\t    pub path: Option<String>,\n387\t    /// A code snippet showing the problematic segment\n388\t    pub snippet: Option<String>,\n389\t    /// Additional context about the error\n390\t    pub context: String,\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\timpl ParseError {\n394\t    /// Create a new ParseError with the given kind\n395\t    ///\n396\t    /// This is the base constructor for creating a ParseError. All fields except\n397\t    /// `kind` are set to their default values (None or empty). Use the builder methods\n398\t    /// to add additional context and location information.\n399\t    ///\n400\t    /// # Examples\n401\t    ///\n402\t    /// ```\n403\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, ParseErrorKind};\n404\t    ///\n405\t    /// let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof);\n406\t    /// assert!(matches!(error.kind, ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n407\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, None);\n408\t    /// ```\n409\t    ///\n410\t    /// For common error types, prefer using the convenience constructors:\n411\t    /// - [`ParseError::syntax()`] for syntax errors\n412\t    /// - [`ParseError::io()`] for I/O errors\n413\t    /// - [`ParseError::validation()`] for validation errors\n414\t    /// - [`ParseError::type_mismatch()`] for type mismatch errors\n415\t    pub fn new(kind: ParseErrorKind) -> Self {\n416\t        Self {\n417\t            kind,\n418\t            line: None,\n419\t            column: None,\n420\t            path: None,\n421\t            snippet: None,\n422\t            context: String::new(),\n423\t        }\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    /// Set the line number for this error\n427\t    ///\n428\t    /// Line numbers are 1-indexed, matching typical text editor conventions.\n429\t    ///\n430\t    /// # Examples\n431\t    ///\n432\t    /// ```\n433\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n434\t    ///\n435\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n436\t    ///     .with_line(42);\n437\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(42));\n438\t    /// ```\n439\t    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n440\t        self.line = Some(line);\n441\t        self\n442\t    }\n443\t\n444\t    /// Set the column number for this error\n445\t    ///\n446\t    /// Column numbers are 1-indexed, indicating the character position within the line.\n447\t    ///\n448\t    /// # Examples\n449\t    ///\n450\t    /// ```\n451\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n452\t    ///\n453\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n454\t    ///     .with_column(15);\n455\t    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(15));\n456\t    /// ```\n457\t    pub fn with_column(mut self, column: usize) -> Self {\n458\t        self.column = Some(column);\n459\t        self\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    /// Set the file/source path for this error\n463\t    ///\n464\t    /// The path can be absolute or relative. It's displayed in error messages to help\n465\t    /// users locate the source of the error.\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// # Examples\n468\t    ///\n469\t    /// ```\n470\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n471\t    ///\n472\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\")\n473\t    ///     .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\");\n474\t    /// assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config/services.yaml\".to_string()));\n475\t    /// ```\n476\t    pub fn with_path(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n477\t        self.path = Some(path.into());\n478\t        self\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    /// Set the code snippet for this error\n482\t    ///\n483\t    /// The snippet should contain the relevant lines of source code where the error occurred.\n484\t    /// This is displayed in detailed error reports to help users understand the context.\n485\t    ///\n486\t    /// # Examples\n487\t    ///\n488\t    /// ```\n489\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n490\t    ///\n491\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n492\t    ///     .with_line(5)\n493\t    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  name: web\\n  port: abc\");\n494\t    ///\n495\t    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n496\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"service:\"));\n497\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"port: abc\"));\n498\t    /// ```\n499\t    pub fn with_snippet(mut self, snippet: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n500\t        self.snippet = Some(snippet.into());\n501\t        self\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    /// Set the context message for this error\n505\t    ///\n506\t    /// Context provides additional information about what operation was being performed\n507\t    /// when the error occurred. This helps users understand the broader scenario.\n508\t    ///\n509\t    /// # Examples\n510\t    ///\n511\t    /// ```\n512\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n513\t    ///\n514\t    /// let error = ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\")\n515\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\");\n516\t    ///\n517\t    /// let summary = error.summary();\n518\t    /// assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n519\t    /// ```\n520\t    pub fn with_context(mut self, context: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n521\t        self.context = context.into();\n522\t        self\n523\t    }\n524\t\n525\t    /// Set both line and column for this error\n526\t    ///\n527\t    /// Convenience method for setting location with a single call.\n528\t    ///\n529\t    /// # Examples\n530\t    ///\n531\t    /// ```\n532\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n533\t    ///\n534\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n535\t    ///     .with_location(10, 25);\n536\t    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n537\t    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(25));\n538\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"10:25\");\n539\t    /// ```\n540\t    pub fn with_location(mut self, line: usize, column: usize) -> Self {\n541\t        self.line = Some(line);\n542\t        self.column = Some(column);\n543\t        self\n544\t    }\n545\t\n546\t    /// Get a formatted location string (e.g., \"file.yaml:10:5\" or \"<unknown>:10\")\n547\t    ///\n548\t    /// This method generates a human-readable location string based on the available\n549\t    /// location information (path, line, column). The format adapts based on which fields are set.\n550\t    ///\n551\t    /// # Examples\n552\t    ///\n553\t    /// ```\n554\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n555\t    ///\n556\t    /// // Full location with path, line, and column\n557\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n558\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n559\t    ///     .with_location(10, 5);\n560\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml:10:5\");\n561\t    ///\n562\t    /// // Line and column only\n563\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n564\t    ///     .with_location(42, 15);\n565\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"42:15\");\n566\t    ///\n567\t    /// // Path only\n568\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n569\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\");\n570\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml\");\n571\t    ///\n572\t    /// // Unknown location\n573\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\");\n574\t    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"<unknown>\");\n575\t    /// ```\n576\t    pub fn location_string(&self) -> String {\n577\t        match (&self.path, self.line, self.column) {\n578\t            (Some(path), Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}:{}\", path, line, col),\n579\t            (Some(path), Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}:{}\", path, line),\n580\t            (Some(path), None, Some(col)) => format!(\"{}::{}\", path, col),\n581\t            (Some(path), None, None) => path.clone(),\n582\t            (None, Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}\", line, col),\n583\t            (None, Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}\", line),\n584\t            (None, None, Some(col)) => format!(\"col {}\", col),\n585\t            (None, None, None) => \"<unknown>\".to_string(),\n586\t        }\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    /// Get a brief summary of the error (single line, suitable for logging)\n590\t    ///\n591\t    /// The summary includes location, error kind, and context (if set). This is ideal\n592\t    /// for logging purposes where a compact, single-line error message is desired.\n593\t    ///\n594\t    /// # Examples\n595\t    ///\n596\t    /// ```\n597\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n598\t    ///\n599\t    /// // Summary with context\n600\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n601\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n602\t    ///     .with_line(10)\n603\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing services\");\n604\t    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n605\t    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token - while parsing services\");\n606\t    ///\n607\t    /// // Summary without context\n608\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n609\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n610\t    ///     .with_line(10);\n611\t    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n612\t    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token\");\n613\t    /// ```\n614\t    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n615\t        let location = self.location_string();\n616\t        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n617\t            format!(\"{}: {} - {}\", location, self.kind, self.context)\n618\t        } else {\n619\t            format!(\"{}: {}\", location, self.kind)\n620\t        }\n621\t    }\n622\t\n623\t    /// Get a detailed multi-line error report with snippet and visual indicator\n624\t    ///\n625\t    /// This method generates a comprehensive error report suitable for displaying\n626\t    /// to users. It includes the error summary, context (if set), and a code snippet\n627\t    /// with a visual indicator (^) pointing to the error location.\n628\t    ///\n629\t    /// # Examples\n630\t    ///\n631\t    /// ```\n632\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n633\t    ///\n634\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n635\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n636\t    ///     .with_line(5)\n637\t    ///     .with_column(10)\n638\t    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing service configuration\")\n639\t    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  port: abc\");\n640\t    ///\n641\t    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n642\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"error:\"));\n643\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"config.yaml:5:10\"));\n644\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"syntax error: invalid port value\"));\n645\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"while parsing service configuration\"));\n646\t    /// assert!(report.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n647\t    /// ```\n648\t    pub fn detailed_report(&self) -> String {\n649\t        let mut report = String::new();\n650\t\n651\t        // Header line with location and error kind\n652\t        report.push_str(&format!(\"error: {}\\n\", self.summary()));\n653\t\n654\t        // Add context section if present\n655\t        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n656\t            report.push_str(&format!(\"  context: {}\\n\", self.context));\n657\t        }\n658\t\n659\t        // Add snippet with visual indicator\n660\t        if let Some(snippet) = &self.snippet {\n661\t            report.push_str(\"\\n  snippet:\\n\");\n662\t            for line in snippet.lines() {\n663\t                report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}\\n\", line));\n664\t            }\n665\t\n666\t            // Add visual indicator for column position\n667\t            if let Some(col) = self.column {\n668\t                if col > 0 {\n669\t                    report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}^\\n\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1))));\n670\t                }\n671\t            }\n672\t        }\n673\t\n674\t        report\n675\t    }\n676\t\n677\t    /// Format this error as a structured log entry (JSON-like but readable)\n678\t    ///\n679\t    /// This method produces a structured representation of the error suitable for\n680\t    /// programmatic analysis or structured logging systems.\n681\t    ///\n682\t    /// # Examples\n683\t    ///\n684\t    /// ```\n685\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n686\t    ///\n687\t    /// let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n688\t    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n689\t    ///     .with_line(8)\n690\t    ///     .with_column(10);\n691\t    ///\n692\t    /// let formatted = error.format_structured();\n693\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n694\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"config.yaml:8:10\"));\n695\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"line: Some(8)\"));\n696\t    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"column: Some(10)\"));\n697\t    /// ```\n698\t    pub fn format_structured(&self) -> String {\n699\t        format!(\n700\t            \"ParseError {{ kind: {:?}, location: {}, line: {:?}, column: {:?} }}\",\n701\t            self.kind,\n702\t            self.location_string(),\n703\t            self.line,\n704\t            self.column\n705\t        )\n706\t    }\n707\t\n708\t    /// Create a syntax error\n709\t    pub fn syntax(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n710\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Syntax(msg.into()))\n711\t    }\n712\t\n713\t    /// Create an I/O error\n714\t    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n715\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(msg.into()))\n716\t    }\n717\t\n718\t    /// Create a validation error\n719\t    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n720\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Validation(msg.into()))\n721\t    }\n722\t\n723\t    /// Create a type mismatch error\n724\t    ///\n725\t    /// # Arguments\n726\t    /// * `field` - The field path where the error occurred\n727\t    /// * `expected` - The expected type description\n728\t    /// * `actual` - The actual type that was received\n729\t    pub fn type_mismatch(field: impl Into<String>, expected: impl Into<String>, actual: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n730\t        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch {\n731\t            field: field.into(),\n732\t            expected: expected.into(),\n733\t            actual: actual.into(),\n734\t        })\n735\t    }\n736\t\n737\t    /// Check if this is a syntax error\n738\t    pub fn is_syntax(&self) -> bool {\n739\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Syntax(_))\n740\t    }\n741\t\n742\t    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n743\t    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n744\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Io(_))\n745\t    }\n746\t\n747\t    /// Check if this is a validation error\n748\t    pub fn is_validation(&self) -> bool {\n749\t        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch { .. })\n755\t    }\n756\t}\n757\t\n758\timpl fmt::Display for ParseError {\n759\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n760\t        // Use the summary method for concise display\n761\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())?;\n762\t\n763\t        // Add detailed section with snippet if available\n764\t        if let Some(snippet) = &self.snippet {\n765\t            write!(f, \"\\n\\n  snippet:\")?;\n766\t            for line in snippet.lines() {\n767\t                write!(f, \"\\n    {}\", line)?;\n768\t            }\n769\t\n770\t            // Add visual indicator for column position\n771\t            if let Some(col) = self.column {\n772\t                if col > 0 {\n773\t                    write!(f, \"\\n    {}\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1)))?;\n774\t                    write!(f, \"^\")?;\n775\t                }\n776\t            }\n777\t        }\n778\t\n779\t        Ok(())\n780\t    }\n781\t}\n782\t\n783\timpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n784\t\n785\timpl PartialEq for ParseError {\n786\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n787\t        // Only compare core error identification fields\n788\t        // Context and snippet are NOT included in equality\n789\t        self.kind == other.kind\n790\t            && self.line == other.line\n791\t            && self.column == other.column\n792\t            && self.path == other.path\n793\t    }\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\timpl fmt::Debug for ParseError {\n797\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> .field(\"line\", &self.line)\n802\t            .field(\"column\", &self.column)\n803\t            .field(\"path\", &self.path)\n804\t            .field(\"context\", &self.context)\n805\t            .field(\"has_snippet\", &self.snippet.is_some())\n806\t            .finish()\n807\t    }\n808\t}\n809\t\n810\t/// The kind of parse error that occurred\n811\t///\n812\t/// This enum represents the core categories of errors that can occur during\n813\t/// YAML parsing operations. Each variant represents a distinct class of error.\n814\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n815\tpub enum ParseErrorKind {\n816\t    /// Syntax error in the YAML source\n817\t    ///\n818\t    /// This variant covers errors related to invalid YAML structure, malformed\n819\t    /// syntax, or violations of the YAML grammar rules.\n820\t    Syntax(String),\n821\t\n822\t    /// I/O error (file not found, permission denied, etc.)\n823\t    ///\n824\t    /// This variant covers errors related to file system operations such as\n825\t    /// reading, writing, or accessing files.\n826\t    Io(String),\n827\t\n828\t    /// Validation error (constraint violations)\n829\t    ///\n830\t    /// This variant covers errors related to constraint violations such as\n831\t    /// required field violations, range violations, or other schema validation failures.\n832\t    Validation(String),\n833\t\n834\t    /// Type mismatch error (unexpected type for a field)\n835\t    ///\n836\t    /// This variant covers errors where a value has an unexpected type, such as\n837\t    /// expecting a string but receiving a number, or expecting a sequence but receiving a scalar.\n838\t    TypeMismatch {\n839\t        /// The field path where the error occurred\n840\t        field: String,\n841\t        /// The expected type\n842\t        expected: String,\n843\t        /// The actual type that was received\n844\t        actual: String,\n845\t    },\n846\t\n847\t    /// Unexpected end of input\n848\t    ///\n849\t    /// This variant covers errors where the YAML source ends prematurely\n850\t    /// and cannot be fully parsed.\n851\t    UnexpectedEof,\n852\t\n853\t    /// Invalid UTF-8 encoding\n854\t    ///\n855\t    /// This variant covers errors where the input contains invalid UTF-8 sequences.\n856\t    InvalidUtf8,\n857\t\n858\t    /// Unknown anchor or alias\n859\t    ///\n860\t    /// This variant covers errors where an anchor or alias reference cannot be resolved.\n861\t    UnknownAnchor(String),\n862\t\n863\t    /// Duplicate key in mapping\n864\t    ///\n865\t    /// This variant covers errors where duplicate keys are found in a YAML mapping.\n866\t    DuplicateKey(String),\n867\t\n868\t    /// Other error\n869\t    ///\n870\t    /// This variant provides a catch-all for errors that don't fit into any of the\n871\t    /// other specific categories, allowing for extensibility.\n872\t    Other(String),\n873\t}\n874\t\n875\timpl fmt::Display for ParseErrorKind {\n876\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n877\t        match self {\n878\t            Self::Syntax(msg) => write!(f, \"syntax error: {}\", msg),\n879\t            Self::Io(msg) => write!(f, \"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n880\t            Self::Validation(msg) => write!(f, \"validation error: {}\", msg),\n881\t            Self::TypeMismatch { field, expected, actual } => {\n882\t                write!(f, \"type mismatch at '{}': expected {}, got {}\", field, expected, actual)\n883\t            }\n884\t            Self::UnexpectedEof => write!(f, \"unexpected end of input\"),\n885\t            Self::InvalidUtf8 => write!(f, \"invalid UTF-8 encoding\"),\n886\t            Self::UnknownAnchor(name) => write!(f, \"unknown anchor: {}\", name),\n887\t            Self::DuplicateKey(key) => write!(f, \"duplicate key: {}\", key),\n888\t            Self::Other(msg) => write!(f, \"error: {}\", msg),\n889\t        }\n890\t    }\n891\t}\n892\t\n893\t/// Result type alias for parse operations\n894\tpub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ParseError>;\n895\t\n896\t// ============================================================================\n897\t// From implementations for error propagation\n898\t// ============================================================================\n899\t\n900\timpl From<std::io::Error> for ParseError {\n901\t    fn from(err: std::io::Error) -> Self {\n902\t        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(err.to_string()))\n903\t    }\n904\t}\n905\t\n906\timpl From<serde_yaml::Error> for ParseError {\n907\t    fn from(err: serde_yaml::Error) -> Self {\n908\t        // kind = if err_msg.contains(\"syntax\") || err_msg.contains(\"unexpected\") || err_msg.contains(\"expected\") {\n913\t            ParseErrorKind::Syntax(err.to_string())\n914\t        } else if err_msg.contains(\"duplicate\") {\n915\t            ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey(err.to_string())\n916\t        } else if err_msg.contains(\"io\") || err_msg.contains(\"failed to read\") {\n917\t            ParseErrorKind::Io(err.to_string())\n918\t        } else {\n919\t            ParseErrorKind::Other(err.to_string())\n920\t        };\n921\t\n922\t        ParseError::new(kind)\n923\t    }\n924\t}\n925\t\n926\timpl From<std::str::Utf8Error> for ParseError {\n927\t    fn from(err: std::str::Utf8Error) -> Self {\n928\t        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8).with_context(err.to_string())\n929\t    }\n930\t}\n931\t\n932\timpl From<std::string::FromUtf8Error> for ParseError {\n933\t    fn from(err:
