}\n936\t}\n937\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e10dfe88-10a1-4c37-bea5-6d2a8b395430","uuid":"cd14ba78-ae45-40d9-8602-293a926c97f4","timestamp":"2026-07-12T23:29:38.029Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/error.rs","content":"//! Error types for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the error types used throughout the YAML parser.\n//!\n//! # Error Handling Philosophy\n//!\n//! The `ParseError` type follows a structured approach to error handling that emphasizes:\n//!\n//! 1. **Clear categorization** - Each error falls into a distinct category (syntax, I/O, validation, etc.)\n//! 2. **Rich context** - Errors carry location information, context messages, and code snippets\n//! 3. **Composability** - Errors propagate cleanly through the call stack using Rust's `?` operator\n//! 4. **User-friendly output** - Multiple formatting options for different use cases (logging, UI, debugging)\n//!\n//! ## When to Use Each Variant\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Io` vs Other Variants\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`] for **file system and I/O operation failures**:\n//! - File not found (`std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound`)\n//! - Permission denied (`std::io::ErrorKind::PermissionDenied`)\n//! - Read/write failures (`std::io::ErrorKind::Other`)\n//! - Network I/O errors (if applicable)\n//!\n//! **Do not use** `Io` for:\n//! - YAML syntax errors → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n//! - Type mismatches → use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n//! - Constraint violations → use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`]\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use Io for file system errors\n//! let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → Io\n//!\n//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use Io for parsing errors\n//! // let error = ParseError::io(\"invalid YAML structure\");  // Wrong! Use Syntax instead\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8` vs Other Variants\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`] specifically for **encoding errors**:\n//! - Invalid UTF-8 byte sequences in input\n//! - Failed UTF-8 validation when converting bytes to strings\n//!\n//! **Do not use** `InvalidUtf8` for:\n//! - I/O errors during file reading → use [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n//! - General parsing failures → use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use InvalidUtf8 for encoding errors\n//! let string = String::from_utf8(bytes)?;  // FromUtf8Error → InvalidUtf8\n//!\n//! // ❌ Incorrect: Don't use InvalidUtf8 for file reading errors\n//! // let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8);  // Wrong! Use Io instead\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof` vs `ParseErrorKind::Syntax`\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof`] when **input ends prematurely**:\n//! - Incomplete YAML documents (missing closing brackets, braces, quotes)\n//! - Truncated files or streams\n//! - Multi-document YAML streams that end mid-document\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`] for **general YAML syntax violations**:\n//! - Invalid indentation\n//! - Invalid escape sequences\n//! - Malformed scalars or mappings\n//! - Any YAML grammar violation that's not specifically EOF-related\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use UnexpectedEof for incomplete input\n//! if input.ends_with(\"key: \") {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n//! }\n//!\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use Syntax for general YAML errors\n//! if !is_valid_indentation(line) {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid indentation\"));\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`] when **a value has the wrong Rust/YAML type**:\n//! - Expecting an integer but finding a string\n//! - Expecting a sequence but finding a scalar\n//! - Expecting a boolean but finding a number\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **semantic constraint violations**:\n//! - Value out of allowed range (e.g., port number > 65535)\n//! - String doesn't match required pattern (e.g., invalid email format)\n//! - Array length constraints violated\n//! - Business logic or schema validation failures\n//!\n//! The key distinction: `TypeMismatch` is about **type**, `Validation` is about **value**.\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use TypeMismatch for type errors\n//! let port = value[\"port\"].as_i64()\n//!     .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\"))?;\n//!\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for value constraints\n//! if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey` vs `ParseErrorKind::Validation`\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey`] specifically for **duplicate key errors**:\n//! - YAML mappings with repeated keys (YAML 1.2 spec violation)\n//! - Configuration files with conflicting entries\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Validation`] for **general validation failures** including:\n//! - Missing required fields (not duplicated, but absent)\n//! - Inter-field constraint violations (e.g., \"start_time > end_time\")\n//! - Schema validation failures beyond type/duplicate checking\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use DuplicateKey for duplicate keys\n//! if keys.contains(&key) {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey(key)));\n//! }\n//!\n//! // ✅ Correct: Use Validation for missing required fields\n//! if value[\"name\"].is_null() {\n//!     return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: 'name'\"));\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ### `ParseErrorKind::Other` (Catch-all) vs Specific Variants\n//!\n//! Use [`ParseErrorKind::Other`] **only as a last resort** for errors that don't fit other categories:\n//! - Unclassifiable serde_yaml errors\n//! - Errors from external libraries that don't map cleanly\n//! - Temporary error cases during refactoring\n//!\n//! **Prefer specific variants** whenever possible:\n//! - Unknown anchors → [`ParseErrorKind::UnknownAnchor`]\n//! - Syntax errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Syntax`]\n//! - I/O errors → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n//! - Type errors → [`ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch`]\n//!\n//! If you find yourself using `Other` frequently, consider adding a new specific variant.\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! // ✅ Prefer specific variants\n//! return Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\"));\n//!\n//! // ⚠️ Use Other only as a catch-all\n//! return Err(ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Other(\"unclassified error\".to_string())));\n//! ```\n//!\n//! # Error Propagation Strategy\n//!\n//! The `ParseError` type is designed to work seamlessly with Rust's error propagation\n//! mechanisms. It implements `From` for common error types, enabling the use of the `?`\n//! operator for automatic error conversion.\n//!\n//! ## Basic Propagation with `?`\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n//!\n//! fn parse_config(path: &Path) -> Result<Config> {\n//!     let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;  // io::Error → ParseError\n//!     parse_yaml(&content)\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Adding Context with `.context()`\n//!\n//! Use the builder-style `.context()` method to add contextual information:\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! fn parse_database_config(value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n//!     let port = value[\"port\"]\n//!         .as_i64()\n//!         .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"null\"))\n//!         .context(\"while parsing database configuration\")?;\n//!\n//!     Ok(DatabaseConfig { port })\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Converting from Other Error Types\n//!\n//! `ParseError` implements `From` for:\n//! - `std::io::Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::Io`]\n//! - `serde_yaml::Error` → Appropriate [`ParseErrorKind`] based on error classification\n//! - `std::str::Utf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n//! - `std::string::FromUtf8Error` → [`ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8`]\n//!\n//! ## Custom Error Creation\n//!\n//! For domain-specific errors, create them directly:\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! fn validate_port(port: i64) -> Result<u16> {\n//!     if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n//!         return Err(ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n//!     }\n//!     Ok(port as u16)\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ## Basic Error Creation\n//!\n//! Creating errors using convenience constructors:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//!\n//! // Create a syntax error\n//! let syntax_err = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML indentation\");\n//! assert!(syntax_err.is_syntax());\n//!\n//! // Create a validation error\n//! let validation_err = ParseError::validation(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\");\n//! assert!(validation_err.is_validation());\n//!\n//! // Create a type mismatch error\n//! let type_err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n//! assert!(type_err.is_type_mismatch());\n//! assert_eq!(type_err.summary(), \"<unknown>: type mismatch at 'port': expected integer, got string\");\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Error Propagation with `?`\n//!\n//! Using the `?` operator to automatically convert errors:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\n//! use std::fs;\n//!\n//! fn read_config(path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n//!     // io::Error is automatically converted to ParseError via From impl\n//!     let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n//!     Ok(content)\n//! }\n//!\n//! // This function demonstrates automatic error conversion\n//! fn parse_config_size(path: &str) -> Result<usize> {\n//!     let content = read_config(path)?;  // ParseError propagates via ?\n//!     Ok(content.len())\n//! }\n//!\n//! // Example: successful read\n//! assert!(parse_config_size(\"/dev/null\").is_ok());\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Custom Error Handling with Builder Pattern\n//!\n//! Using builder methods to add context and location information:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//!\n//! fn validate_service_config(yaml_content: &str) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n//!     // Simulating an error during validation\n//!     if yaml_content.contains(\"port: abc\") {\n//!         let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"service.port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n//!             .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\")\n//!             .with_line(5)\n//!             .with_column(10)\n//!             .with_context(\"while validating service configuration\")\n//!             .with_snippet(\"services:\\n  - name: web\\n    port: abc\");\n//!\n//!         return Err(error);\n//!     }\n//!     Ok(())\n//! }\n//!\n//! // Create an error to verify the builder pattern\n//! let error = ParseError::validation(\"invalid port\")\n//!     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n//!     .with_line(10)\n//!     .with_column(5)\n//!     .with_context(\"while parsing configuration\");\n//!\n//! assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n//! assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n//! assert_eq!(error.column, Some(5));\n//! assert_eq!(error.context, \"while parsing configuration\");\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Error Display and Formatting\n//!\n//! Different ways to format and display errors:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//!\n//! // Create an error with full context\n//! let error = ParseError::syntax(\"unexpected colon\")\n//!     .with_path(\"config/database.yaml\")\n//!     .with_line(15)\n//!     .with_column(8)\n//!     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\")\n//!     .with_snippet(\"database:\\n  host: localhost\\n  port: 5432:\\n    invalid\");\n//!\n//! // Get a single-line summary (good for logging)\n//! let summary = error.summary();\n//! assert!(summary.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15\"));\n//! assert!(summary.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n//! assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n//!\n//! // Get a detailed multi-line report (good for user display)\n//! let detailed = error.detailed_report();\n//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"error:\"));\n//! assert!(detailed.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n//!\n//! // Get a structured representation (good for debugging)\n//! let structured = error.format_structured();\n//! assert!(structured.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n//! assert!(structured.contains(\"config/database.yaml:15:8\"));\n//! assert!(structured.contains(\"line: Some(15)\"));\n//!\n//! // Display implementation provides user-friendly output\n//! let display_string = format!(\"{}\", error);\n//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"config/database.yaml\"));\n//! assert!(display_string.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Error Conversion from Standard Types\n//!\n//! Automatic conversion from standard library errors:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//! use std::io;\n//!\n//! // io::Error converts to ParseError::Io\n//! let io_err = io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::NotFound, \"file not found\");\n//! let parse_err: ParseError = io_err.into();\n//! assert!(parse_err.is_io());\n//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"I/O error\"));\n//! assert!(parse_err.summary().contains(\"file not found\"));\n//!\n//! // String::from_utf8 Error converts to ParseError::InvalidUtf8\n//! let invalid_bytes = b\"\\xff\\xfe\\xfd\";\n//! let utf8_err = String::from_utf8(invalid_bytes.to_vec()).unwrap_err();\n//! let parse_err: ParseError = utf8_err.into();\n//! assert!(matches!(parse_err.kind, armor::parsers::yaml::ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8));\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Working with Error Types\n//!\n//! Checking error types and handling specific cases:\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n//!\n//! let errors = vec![\n//!     ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\"),\n//!     ParseError::io(\"file not found\"),\n//!     ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\"),\n//!     ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"string\", \"integer\"),\n//! ];\n//!\n//! // Check specific error types\n//! assert!(errors[0].is_syntax());\n//! assert!(errors[1].is_io());\n//! assert!(errors[2].is_validation());\n//! assert!(errors[3].is_type_mismatch());\n//!\n//! // Filter errors by type\n//! let syntax_errors: Vec<_> = errors.iter()\n//!     .filter(|e| e.is_syntax())\n//!     .collect();\n//! assert_eq!(syntax_errors.len(), 1);\n//! ```\n\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Main error type for YAML parsing operations\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct ParseError {\n    /// The kind of error that occurred\n    pub kind: ParseErrorKind,\n    /// The line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line: Option<usize>,\n    /// The column number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub column: Option<usize>,\n    /// The file or source path where the error occurred\n    pub path: Option<String>,\n    /// A code snippet showing the problematic segment\n    pub snippet: Option<String>,\n    /// Additional context about the error\n    pub context: String,\n}\n\nimpl ParseError {\n    /// Create a new ParseError with the given kind\n    ///\n    /// This is the base constructor for creating a ParseError. All fields except\n    /// `kind` are set to their default values (None or empty). Use the builder methods\n    /// to add additional context and location information.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, ParseErrorKind};\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof);\n    /// assert!(matches!(error.kind, ParseErrorKind::UnexpectedEof));\n    /// assert_eq!(error.line, None);\n    /// ```\n    ///\n    /// For common error types, prefer using the convenience constructors:\n    /// - [`ParseError::syntax()`] for syntax errors\n    /// - [`ParseError::io()`] for I/O errors\n    /// - [`ParseError::validation()`] for validation errors\n    /// - [`ParseError::type_mismatch()`] for type mismatch errors\n    pub fn new(kind: ParseErrorKind) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            kind,\n            line: None,\n            column: None,\n            path: None,\n            snippet: None,\n            context: String::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the line number for this error\n    ///\n    /// Line numbers are 1-indexed, matching typical text editor conventions.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_line(42);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(42));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the column number for this error\n    ///\n    /// Column numbers are 1-indexed, indicating the character position within the line.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_column(15);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(15));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_column(mut self, column: usize) -> Self {\n        self.column = Some(column);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the file/source path for this error\n    ///\n    /// The path can be absolute or relative. It's displayed in error messages to help\n    /// users locate the source of the error.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\");\n    /// assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config/services.yaml\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_path(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.path = Some(path.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the code snippet for this error\n    ///\n    /// The snippet should contain the relevant lines of source code where the error occurred.\n    /// This is displayed in detailed error reports to help users understand the context.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n    ///     .with_line(5)\n    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  name: web\\n  port: abc\");\n    ///\n    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"service:\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"port: abc\"));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_snippet(mut self, snippet: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.snippet = Some(snippet.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the context message for this error\n    ///\n    /// Context provides additional information about what operation was being performed\n    /// when the error occurred. This helps users understand the broader scenario.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\")\n    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\");\n    ///\n    /// let summary = error.summary();\n    /// assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing database configuration\"));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_context(mut self, context: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.context = context.into();\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set both line and column for this error\n    ///\n    /// Convenience method for setting location with a single call.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_location(10, 25);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n    /// assert_eq!(error.column, Some(25));\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"10:25\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_location(mut self, line: usize, column: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self.column = Some(column);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get a formatted location string (e.g., \"file.yaml:10:5\" or \"<unknown>:10\")\n    ///\n    /// This method generates a human-readable location string based on the available\n    /// location information (path, line, column). The format adapts based on which fields are set.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// // Full location with path, line, and column\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_location(10, 5);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml:10:5\");\n    ///\n    /// // Line and column only\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n    ///     .with_location(42, 15);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"42:15\");\n    ///\n    /// // Path only\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\");\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"config.yaml\");\n    ///\n    /// // Unknown location\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"test\");\n    /// assert_eq!(error.location_string(), \"<unknown>\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn location_string(&self) -> String {\n        match (&self.path, self.line, self.column) {\n            (Some(path), Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}:{}\", path, line, col),\n            (Some(path), Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}:{}\", path, line),\n            (Some(path), None, Some(col)) => format!(\"{}::{}\", path, col),\n            (Some(path), None, None) => path.clone(),\n            (None, Some(line), Some(col)) => format!(\"{}:{}\", line, col),\n            (None, Some(line), None) => format!(\"{}\", line),\n            (None, None, Some(col)) => format!(\"col {}\", col),\n            (None, None, None) => \"<unknown>\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a brief summary of the error (single line, suitable for logging)\n    ///\n    /// The summary includes location, error kind, and context (if set). This is ideal\n    /// for logging purposes where a compact, single-line error message is desired.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// // Summary with context\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_line(10)\n    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing services\");\n    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token - while parsing services\");\n    ///\n    /// // Summary without context\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_line(10);\n    /// assert_eq!(error.summary(),\n    ///     \"config.yaml:10: syntax error: invalid token\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n        let location = self.location_string();\n        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n            format!(\"{}: {} - {}\", location, self.kind, self.context)\n        } else {\n            format!(\"{}: {}\", location, self.kind)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a detailed multi-line error report with snippet and visual indicator\n    ///\n    /// This method generates a comprehensive error report suitable for displaying\n    /// to users. It includes the error summary, context (if set), and a code snippet\n    /// with a visual indicator (^) pointing to the error location.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid port value\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_line(5)\n    ///     .with_column(10)\n    ///     .with_context(\"while parsing service configuration\")\n    ///     .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  port: abc\");\n    ///\n    /// let report = error.detailed_report();\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"error:\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"config.yaml:5:10\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"syntax error: invalid port value\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"while parsing service configuration\"));\n    /// assert!(report.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn detailed_report(&self) -> String {\n        let mut report = String::new();\n\n        // Header line with location and error kind\n        report.push_str(&format!(\"error: {}\\n\", self.summary()));\n\n        // Add context section if present\n        if !self.context.is_empty() {\n            report.push_str(&format!(\"  context: {}\\n\", self.context));\n        }\n\n        // Add snippet with visual indicator\n        if let Some(snippet) = &self.snippet {\n            report.push_str(\"\\n  snippet:\\n\");\n            for line in snippet.lines() {\n                report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}\\n\", line));\n            }\n\n            // Add visual indicator for column position\n            if let Some(col) = self.column {\n                if col > 0 {\n                    report.push_str(&format!(\"    {}^\\n\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1))));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        report\n    }\n\n    /// Format this error as a structured log entry (JSON-like but readable)\n    ///\n    /// This method produces a structured representation of the error suitable for\n    /// programmatic analysis or structured logging systems.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n    ///     .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n    ///     .with_line(8)\n    ///     .with_column(10);\n    ///\n    /// let formatted = error.format_structured();\n    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"config.yaml:8:10\"));\n    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"line: Some(8)\"));\n    /// assert!(formatted.contains(\"column: Some(10)\"));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn format_structured(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\n            \"ParseError {{ kind: {:?}, location: {}, line: {:?}, column: {:?} }}\",\n            self.kind,\n            self.location_string(),\n            self.line,\n            self.column\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Create a syntax error\n    pub fn syntax(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Syntax(msg.into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Create an I/O error\n    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(msg.into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Create a validation error\n    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::Validation(msg.into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Create a type mismatch error\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `field` - The field path where the error occurred\n    /// * `expected` - The expected type description\n    /// * `actual` - The actual type that was received\n    pub fn type_mismatch(field: impl Into<String>, expected: impl Into<String>, actual: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch {\n            field: field.into(),\n            expected: expected.into(),\n            actual: actual.into(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a syntax error\n    pub fn is_syntax(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Syntax(_))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Io(_))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a validation error\n    pub fn is_validation(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::Validation(_))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a type mismatch error\n    pub fn is_type_mismatch(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, ParseErrorKind::TypeMismatch { .. })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ParseError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        // Use the summary method for concise display\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())?;\n\n        // Add detailed section with snippet if available\n        if let Some(snippet) = &self.snippet {\n            write!(f, \"\\n\\n  snippet:\")?;\n            for line in snippet.lines() {\n                write!(f, \"\\n    {}\", line)?;\n            }\n\n            // Add visual indicator for column position\n            if let Some(col) = self.column {\n                if col > 0 {\n                    write!(f, \"\\n    {}\", \" \".repeat(col.saturating_sub(1)))?;\n                    write!(f, \"^\")?;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n\nimpl PartialEq for ParseError {\n    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        // Only compare core error identification fields\n        // Context and snippet are NOT included in equality\n        self.kind == other.kind\n            && self.line == other.line\n            && self.column == other.column\n            && self.path == other.path\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Debug for ParseError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"ParseError\")\n            .field(\"kind\", &self.kind)\n            .field(\"location\", &self.location_string())\n            .field(\"line\", &self.line)\n            .field(\"column\", &self.column)\n            .field(\"path\", &self.path)\n            .field(\"context\", &self.context)\n            .field(\"has_snippet\", &self.snippet.is_some())\n            .finish()\n    }\n}\n\n/// The kind of parse error that occurred\n///\n/// This enum represents the core categories of errors that can occur during\n/// YAML parsing operations. Each variant represents a distinct class of error.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\npub enum ParseErrorKind {\n    /// Syntax error in the YAML source\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors related to invalid YAML structure, malformed\n    /// syntax, or violations of the YAML grammar rules.\n    Syntax(String),\n\n    /// I/O error (file not found, permission denied, etc.)\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors related to file system operations such as\n    /// reading, writing, or accessing files.\n    Io(String),\n\n    /// Validation error (constraint violations)\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors related to constraint violations such as\n    /// required field violations, range violations, or other schema validation failures.\n    Validation(String),\n\n    /// Type mismatch error (unexpected type for a field)\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where a value has an unexpected type, such as\n    /// expecting a string but receiving a number, or expecting a sequence but receiving a scalar.\n    TypeMismatch {\n        /// The field path where the error occurred\n        field: String,\n        /// The expected type\n        expected: String,\n        /// The actual type that was received\n        actual: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Unexpected end of input\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where the YAML source ends prematurely\n    /// and cannot be fully parsed.\n    UnexpectedEof,\n\n    /// Invalid UTF-8 encoding\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where the input contains invalid UTF-8 sequences.\n    InvalidUtf8,\n\n    /// Unknown anchor or alias\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where an anchor or alias reference cannot be resolved.\n    UnknownAnchor(String),\n\n    /// Duplicate key in mapping\n    ///\n    /// This variant covers errors where duplicate keys are found in a YAML mapping.\n    DuplicateKey(String),\n\n    /// Other error\n    ///\n    /// This variant provides a catch-all for errors that don't fit into any of the\n    /// other specific categories, allowing for extensibility.\n    Other(String),\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ParseErrorKind {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Syntax(msg) => write!(f, \"syntax error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::Io(msg) => write!(f, \"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::Validation(msg) => write!(f, \"validation error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::TypeMismatch { field, expected, actual } => {\n                write!(f, \"type mismatch at '{}': expected {}, got {}\", field, expected, actual)\n            }\n            Self::UnexpectedEof => write!(f, \"unexpected end of input\"),\n            Self::InvalidUtf8 => write!(f, \"invalid UTF-8 encoding\"),\n            Self::UnknownAnchor(name) => write!(f, \"unknown anchor: {}\", name),\n            Self::DuplicateKey(key) => write!(f, \"duplicate key: {}\", key),\n            Self::Other(msg) => write!(f, \"error: {}\", msg),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result type alias for parse operations\npub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ParseError>;\n\n// ============================================================================\n// From implementations for error propagation\n// ============================================================================\n\nimpl From<std::io::Error> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: std::io::Error) -> Self {\n        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::Io(err.to_string()))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<serde_yaml::Error> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: serde_yaml::Error) -> Self {\n        // Classify serde_yaml errors into appropriate ParseError kinds\n        // based on the error message content\n        let err_msg = err.to_string().to_lowercase();\n\n        let kind = if err_msg.contains(\"syntax\") || err_msg.contains(\"unexpected\") || err_msg.contains(\"expected\") {\n            ParseErrorKind::Syntax(err.to_string())\n        } else if err_msg.contains(\"duplicate\") {\n            ParseErrorKind::DuplicateKey(err.to_string())\n        } else if err_msg.contains(\"io\") || err_msg.contains(\"failed to read\") {\n            ParseErrorKind::Io(err.to_string())\n        } else {\n            ParseErrorKind::Other(err.to_string())\n        };\n\n        ParseError::new(kind)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<std::str::Utf8Error> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: std::str::Utf8Error) -> Self {\n        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8).with_context(err.to_string())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<std::string::FromUtf8Error> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: std::string::FromUtf8Error) -> Self {\n        ParseError::new(ParseErrorKind::InvalidUtf8).with_context(err.to_string())\n    }\n}\n","numLines":937,"startLine":1,"totalLines":937}}}

processing and schema validation, following the bf-68hqo\n14\t/// error hierarchy design.\n15\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n16\tpub enum ErrorType {\n17\t    /// Syntax errors in YAML VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES,\n110\t    /// Object has too many properties\n111\t    VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES,\n112\t\n113\t    // ========== I/O Errors ==========\n114\t    /// EOF_UNEXPECTED,\n134\t}\n135\t\n136\timpl ErrorCode {\n137\t    /// Get the error type category for this error code\n138\t    pub fn error_type(&self) -> ErrorType {\n139\t        match self {\n140\t            // Syntax errors\n141\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_SYNTAX | Self::YAML_INVALID_INDENTATION |\n142\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_DELIMITER | Self::YAML_INVALID_ESCAPE_SEQUENCE |\n143\t Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_SHORT | Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_LONG |\n154\t            Self::VALIDATION_PATTERN_MISMATCH | Self::VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE |\n155\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_FEW_ITEMS | Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_MANY_ITEMS |\n156\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_NOT_UNIQUE | Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES |\n157\t            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES => ErrorType::Validation,\n158\t\n159\t            // I/O errors\n160\t            Self::IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND | Self::IO_PERMISSION_DENIED |\n161\t            Self::IO_READ_FAILED | Self::IO_WRITE_FAILED => ErrorType::Io,\n162\t\n163\t            // Encoding errors\n164\t            Self::ENCODING_INVALID_UTF8 => ErrorType::InvalidUtf8,\n165\t\n166\t            // Other errors\n167\t            Self::ANCHOR_UNKNOWN => ErrorType::UnknownAnchor,\n168\t            Self::KEY_DUPLICATE => ErrorType::DuplicateKey,\n169\t            Self::EOF_UNEXPECTED => ErrorType::UnexpectedEof,\n170\t        }\n171\t    }\n172\t\n173\t    /// Get a human-readable description for this error code\n174\t    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n175\t        match self {\n176\t            // Syntax errors\n177\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_SYNTAX => \"Invalid YAML syntax structure\",\n178\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_INDENTATION => \"Invalid YAML indentation\",\n179\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_DELIMITER => \"Invalid YAML delimiter\",\n180\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_ESCAPE_SEQUENCE => \"Invalid escape sequence\",\n181\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_SCALAR => \"Invalid scalar value\",\n182\t\n183\t            // Type mismatches\n184\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_INTEGER => \"Expected integer type\",\n185\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_STRING => \"Expected string type\",\n186\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_BOOLEAN => \"Expected boolean type\",\n187\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_ARRAY => \"Expected array type\",\n188\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_OBJECT => \"Expected object type\",\n189\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_NUMBER => \"Expected number type\",\n190\t            Self::TYPE_UNEXPECTED_NULL => \"Unexpected null value\",\n191\t\n192\t            // Validation errors\n193\t            Self::VALIDATION_REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING => \"Required field is missing\",\n194\t            Self::VALIDATION_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE => \"Value out of allowed range\",\n195\t            Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_SHORT => \"String too short\",\n196\t            Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_LONG => \"String too long\",\n197\t            Self::VALIDATION_PATTERN_MISMATCH => \"Pattern mismatch\",\n198\t            Self::VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE => \"Invalid value\",\n199\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_FEW_ITEMS => \"Array has too few items\",\n200\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_MANY_ITEMS => \"Array has too many items\",\n201\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_NOT_UNIQUE => \"Array items not unique\",\n202\t            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES => \"Object has too few properties\",\n203\t            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES => \"Object has too many properties\",\n204\t\n205\t            // I/O errors\n206\t            Self::IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND => \"File not found\",\n207\t            Self::IO_PERMISSION_DENIED => \"Permission denied\",\n208\t            Self::IO_READ_FAILED => \"Read failed\",\n209\t            Self::IO_WRITE_FAILED => \"Write failed\",\n210\t\n211\t            // Encoding errors\n212\t            Self::ENCODING_INVALID_UTF8 => \"Invalid UTF-8 encoding\",\n213\t\n214\t            // Other errors\n215\t            Self::ANCHOR_UNKNOWN => \"Unknown anchor reference\",\n216\t            Self::KEY_DUPLICATE => \"Duplicate key in mapping\",\n217\t            Self::EOF_UNEXPECTED => \"Unexpected end of input\",\n218\t        }\n219\t    }\n220\t}\n221\t\n222\timpl fmt::Display for ErrorCode {\n223\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n224\t        write!(f, \"{:?}\", self)\n225\t    }\n226\t}\n227\t\n228\t/// Status enum representing success/error states for Result types\n229\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n230\tpub enum Status {\n231\t    /// Operation completed successfully\n232\t    SUCCESS,\n233\t    /// Operation encountered an error\n234\t    ERROR,\n235\t}\n236\t\n237\timpl Status {\n238\t    /// Check if status is SUCCESS\n239\t    pub fn is_success(self) -> bool {\n240\t        matches!(self, Status::SUCCESS)\n241\t    }\n242\t\n243\t    /// Check if status is ERROR\n244\t    pub fn is_error(self) -> bool {\n245\t        matches!(self, Status::ERROR)\n246\t    }\n247\t\n248\t    /// Convert from boolean (true = SUCCESS, false = ERROR)\n249\t    pub fn from_bool(success: bool) -> Self {\n250\t        if success {\n251\t            Status::SUCCESS\n252\t        } else {\n253\t            Status::ERROR\n254\t        }\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    /// Convert to boolean\n258\t    pub fn as_bool(self) -> bool {\n259\t        self.is_success()\n260\t    }\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\timpl fmt::Display for Status {\n264\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n265\t        match self {\n266\t            Status::SUCCESS => write!(f, \"SUCCESS\"),\n267\t            Status::ERROR => write!(f, \"ERROR\"),\n268\t        }\n269\t    }\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t/// Result of a YAML operation with status, data, and error fields\n273\t///\n274\t/// This is a simple dataclass-style result type that holds:\n275\t/// - `status`: A Status enum indicating SUCCESS or ERROR\n276\t/// - `data`: Optional generic data payload\n277\t/// - `error`: Optional error message string\n278\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n279\tpub struct OperationResult<T> {\n280\t    /// The operation status (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n281\t    pub status: Status,\n282\t    /// The parsed content data, if successful\n283\t    pub data: Option<T>,\n284\t    /// The error message, if the operation failed\n285\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\timpl<T> OperationResult<T> {\n289\t    /// Create a new OperationResult with all three fields\n290\t    ///\n291\t    /// # Arguments\n292\t    /// * `status` - The Status enum value (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n293\t    /// * `data` - Optional data payload\n294\t    /// * `error` - Optional error message\n295\t    pub fn new(status: Status, data: Option<T>, error: Option<String>) -> Self {\n296\t        Self { status, data, error }\n297\t    }\n298\t\n299\t    /// Create a successful OperationResult with data\n300\t    ///\n301\t    /// # Arguments\n302\t    /// * `data` - The successful result data\n303\t    pub fn success(data: T) -> Self {\n304\t        Self {\n305\t            status: Status::SUCCESS,\n306\t            data: Some(data),\n307\t            error: None,\n308\t        }\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    /// Create a failed OperationResult with an error message\n312\t    ///\n313\t    /// # Arguments\n314\t    /// * `message` - The error message describing the failure\n315\t    pub fn error(message: String) -> Self {\n316\t        Self {\n317\t            status: Status::ERROR,\n318\t            data: None,\n319\t            error: Some(message),\n320\t        }\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    /// Check if the operation was successful\n324\t    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n325\t        self.status.is_success()\n326\t    }\n327\t\n328\t    /// Check if the operation failed\n329\t    pub fn is_error(&self) -> bool {\n330\t        self.status.is_error()\n331\t    }\n332\t\n333\t    /// Get a reference to the data, if successful\n334\t    pub fn get_data(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n335\t        self.data.as_ref()\n336\t    }\n337\t\n338\t    /// Get the error message as a string slice, if failed\n339\t    pub fn get_error(&self) -> Option<&str> {\n340\t        self.error.as_deref()\n341\t    }\n342\t}\n343\t\n344\t/// Result of a YAML parsing operation\n345\t///\n346\t/// `ParseResult<T>` represents the outcome of a YAML parsing operation, encapsulating\n347\t/// either a successful parse with its associated data and metadata, or a failure with\n348\t/// detailed error information.\n349\t///\n350\t/// # Type Parameter\n351\t///\n352\t/// - `T` - The type of the parsed value. This is typically the target type that the YAML\n353\t///   content is being parsed into (e.g., a configuration struct, `serde_yaml::Value`,\n354\t///   or any deserializable type).\n355\t///\n356\t/// # Fields\n357\t///\n358\t/// ## Core Fields\n359\t///\n360\t/// - `value: Option<T>` - The successfully parsed value. Present when parsing succeeds.\n361\t/// - `error: Option<ParseError>` - Detailed error information. Present when parsing fails.\n362\t/// - `metadata: ParseMetadata` - Metadata about the parsing operation (lines processed,\n363\t///   bytes processed, processing time, source path). Always present.\n364\t/// - `warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>` - Collection of non-fatal warnings that occurred during\n365\t///   parsing. Parsing can succeed with warnings present.\n366\t///\n367\t/// # Design Philosophy\n368\t///\n369\t/// `ParseResult<T>` follows a structured approach to representing parsing outcomes:\n370\t///\n371\t/// 1. **Explicit success/failure states** - Success requires both `value` present and\n372\t///    `error` absent. Failure requires `error` present and `value` absent.\n373\t/// 2. **Rich context** - Both success and failure carry metadata about the operation.\n374\t/// 3. **Warnings without failure** - Warnings are non-fatal; parsing can succeed with\n375\t///    warnings present (e.g., deprecated field usage, unknown keys in lenient mode).\n376\t/// 4. **Composable operations** - Supports `map()` for transforming success values,\n377\t///    and `From<Result<T>>` for interoperability with standard `Result` types.\n378\t///\n379\t/// # Examples\n380\t///\n381\t/// ## Successful Parse\n382\t///\n383\t/// ```ignore\n384\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseMetadata};\n385\t///\n386\t/// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n387\t/// assert!(result.is_success());\n388\t/// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n389\t/// assert!(result.warnings().is_empty());\n390\t/// ```\n391\t///\n392\t/// ## Failed Parse\n393\t///\n394\t/// ```ignore\n395\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n396\t///\n397\t/// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n398\t/// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n399\t/// assert!(result.is_failure());\n400\t/// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n401\t/// ```\n402\t///\n403\t/// ## Parse with Warnings\n404\t///\n405\t/// ```ignore\n406\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n407\t///\n408\t/// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n409\t/// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old_field\", \"new_field\"));\n410\t///\n411\t/// assert!(result.is_success());\n412\t/// assert!(!result.warnings().is_empty());\n413\t/// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n414\t/// ```\n415\t///\n416\t/// ## Mapping Over Success\n417\t///\n418\t/// ```ignore\n419\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n420\t///\n421\t/// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n422\t/// let mapped = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n423\t///\n424\t/// assert_eq!(mapped.value(), Some(&20));\n425\t/// ```\n426\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n427\tpub struct ParseResult<T> {\n428\t    /// The parsed value, if successful\n429\t    value: Option<T>,\n430\t    /// The error, if parsing failed\n431\t    error: Option<ParseError>,\n432\t    /// Additional metadata about the parse operation\n433\t    metadata: ParseMetadata,\n434\t    /// Non-fatal warnings that occurred during parsing\n435\t    warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>,\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\timpl<T> ParseResult<T> {\n439\t    /// Create a successful parse result\n440\t    ///\n441\t    /// # Arguments\n442\t    /// * `value` - The successfully parsed value\n443\t    ///\n444\t    /// # Returns\n445\t    /// A `ParseResult` with the value set, no error, empty warnings, and default metadata\n446\t    ///\n447\t    /// # Examples\n448\t    ///\n449\t    /// ```\n450\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n451\t    ///\n452\t    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n453\t    /// assert!(result.is_success());\n454\t    /// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n455\t    /// ```\n456\t    pub fn success(value: T) -> Self {\n457\t        Self {\n458\t            value: Some(value),\n459\t            error: None,\n460\t            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n461\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n462\t        }\n463\t    }\n464\t\n465\t    /// Create a failed parse result\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// # Arguments\n468\t    /// * `error` - The error that caused parsing to fail\n469\t    ///\n470\t    /// # Returns\n471\t    /// A `ParseResult` with the error set, no value, empty warnings, and default metadata\n472\t    ///\n473\t    /// # Examples\n474\t    ///\n475\t    /// ```\n476\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n477\t    ///\n478\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n479\t    /// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n480\t    /// assert!(result.is_failure());\n481\t    /// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n482\t    /// ```\n483\t    pub fn failure(error: ParseError) -> Self {\n484\t        Self {\n485\t            value: None,\n486\t            error: Some(error),\n487\t            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n488\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n489\t        }\n490\t    }\n491\t\n492\t    /// Check if the parse was successful\n493\t    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n494\t        self.error.is_none() && self.value.is_some()\n495\t    }\n496\t\n497\t    /// Check if the parse failed\n498\t    pub fn is_failure(&self) -> bool {\n499\t        self.error.is_some()\n500\t    }\n501\t\n502\t    /// Get the parsed value\n503\t    ///\n504\t    /// Returns None if the parse failed\n505\t    pub fn value(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n506\t        self.value.as_ref()\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    /// Get the error, if any\n510\t    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&ParseError> {\n511\t        self.error.as_ref()\n512\t    }\n513\t\n514\t    /// Get the metadata for this parse result\n515\t    ///\n516\t    /// # Returns\n517\t    /// A reference to the `ParseMetadata` containing information about lines processed,\n518\t    /// bytes processed, processing time, and source path\n519\t    pub fn metadata(&self) -> &ParseMetadata {\n520\t        &self.metadata\n521\t    }\n522\t\n523\t    /// Get the warnings for this parse result\n524\t    ///\n525\t    /// # Returns\n526\t    /// A slice of `ParseWarning` items representing non-fatal issues that occurred during parsing\n527\t    ///\n528\t    /// # Examples\n529\t    ///\n530\t    /// ```\n531\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n532\t    ///\n533\t    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n534\t    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old\", \"new\"));\n535\t    ///\n536\t    /// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n537\t    /// ```\n538\t    pub fn warnings(&self) -> &[ParseWarning] {\n539\t        &self.warnings\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    /// Check if this parse result has any warnings\n543\t    ///\n544\t    /// # Returns\n545\t    /// `true` if there are warnings present, `false` otherwise\n546\t    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n547\t        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n548\t    }\n549\t\n550\t    /// Add a warning to this parse result\n551\t    ///\n552\t    /// # Arguments\n553\t    /// * `warning` - The warning to add\n554\t    ///\n555\t    /// # Examples\n556\t    ///\n557\t    /// ```\n558\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n559\t    ///\n560\t    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n561\t    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::unknown_key(\"deprecated_field\"));\n562\t    /// assert!(result.has_warnings());\n563\t    /// ```\n564\t    pub fn add_warning(&mut self, warning: ParseWarning) {\n565\t        self.warnings.push(warning);\n566\t    }\n567\t\n568\t    /// Add warnings to this parse result\n569\t    ///\n570\t    /// # Arguments\n571\t    /// * `warnings` - An iterator of warnings to add\n572\t    pub fn add_warnings<I>(&mut self, warnings: I)\n573\t    where\n574\t        I: IntoIterator<Item = ParseWarning>,\n575\t    {\n576\t        self.warnings.extend(warnings);\n577\t    }\n578\t\n579\t    /// Set the metadata for this parse result\n580\t    ///\n581\t    /// # Arguments\n582\t    /// * `metadata` - The metadata to set\n583\t    pub fn with_metadata(mut self, metadata: ParseMetadata) -> Self {\n584\t        self.metadata = metadata;\n585\t        self\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    /// Unwrap the value, consuming the result\n589\t    ///\n590\t    /// # Panics\n591\t    /// Panics if the parse failed (i.e., if `error` is present)\n592\t    ///\n593\t    /// # Examples\n594\t    ///\n595\t    /// ```\n596\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n597\t    ///\n598\t    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n599\t    /// assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), 42);\n600\t    /// ```\n601\t    pub fn unwrap(self) -> T {\n602\t        self.value.expect(\n603\t            \"called unwrap() on a failed ParseResult\"\n604\t        )\n605\t    }\n606\t\n607\t    /// Unwrap the value or return a default\n608\t    ///\n609\t    /// # Arguments\n610\t    /// * `default` - The default value to return if parsing failed\n611\t    ///\n612\t    /// # Returns\n613\t    /// The parsed value if successful, or the provided default\n614\t    ///\n615\t    /// # Examples\n616\t    ///\n617\t    /// ```\n618\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n619\t    ///\n620\t    /// let success: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(42);\n621\t    /// assert_eq!(success.unwrap_or(0), 42);\n622\t    ///\n623\t    /// let failure = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(ParseError::syntax(\"error\"));\n624\t    /// assert_eq!(failure.unwrap_or(0), 0);\n625\t    /// ```\n626\t    pub fn unwrap_or(self, default: T) -> T {\n627\t        self.value.unwrap_or(default)\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    /// Map the success value to a new type\n631\t    ///\n632\t    /// # Type Parameters\n633\t    /// * `U` - The target type after mapping\n634\t    /// * `F` - Function type that transforms `T` into `U`\n635\t    ///\n636\t    /// # Arguments\n637\t    /// * `f` - A function that transforms the success value\n638\t    ///\n639\t    /// # Returns\n640\t    /// A new `ParseResult<U>` with the transformed value, preserving error, metadata, and warnings\n641\t    ///\n642\t    /// # Examples\n643\t    ///\n644\t    /// ```\n645\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n646\t    ///\n647\t    /// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n648\t    /// let doubled = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n649\t    ///\n650\t    /// assert_eq!(doubled.value(), Some(&20));\n651\t    /// ```\n652\t    pub fn map<U, F>(self, f: F) -> ParseResult<U>\n653\t    where\n654\t        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n655\t    {\n656\t        match self.value {\n657\t            Some(v) => ParseResult {\n658\t                value: Some(f(v)),\n659\t                error: None,\n660\t                metadata: self.metadata,\n661\t                warnings: self.warnings,\n662\t            },\n663\t            None => ParseResult {\n664\t                value: None,\n665\t                error: self.error,\n666\t                metadata: self.metadata,\n667\t                warnings: self.warnings,\n668\t            },\n669\t        }\n670\t    }\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\timpl<T> From<Result<T>> for ParseResult<T> {\n674\t    fn from(result: Result<T>) -> Self {\n675\t        match result {\n676\t            Ok(value) => Self::success(value),\n677\t            Err(error) => Self::failure(error),\n678\t        }\n679\t    }\n680\t}\n681\t\n682\t/// Metadata about a parsing operation\n683\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n684\tpub struct ParseMetadata {\n685\t    /// Number of lines processed\n686\t    pub lines_processed: usize,\n687\t    /// Number of bytes processed\n688\t    pub bytes_processed: usize,\n689\t    /// Processing time in nanoseconds\n690\t    pub processing_time_ns: Option<u64>,\n691\t    /// Source file path, if known\n692\t    pub source_path: Option<String>,\n693\t}\n694\t\n695\timpl ParseMetadata {\n696\t    /// Create new metadata\n697\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n698\t        Self::default()\n699\t    }\n700\t\n701\t    /// Set the number of lines processed\n702\t    pub fn with_lines(mut self, lines: usize) -> Self {\n703\t        self.lines_processed = lines;\n704\t        self\n705\t    }\n706\t\n707\t    /// Set the number of bytes processed\n708\t    pub fn with_bytes(mut self, bytes: usize) -> Self {\n709\t        self.bytes_processed = bytes;\n710\t        self\n711\t    }\n712\t\n713\t    /// Set the source path\n714\t    pub fn with_source(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n715\t        self.source_path = Some(path.into());\n716\t        self\n717\t    }\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t/// Result of a YAML validation operation\n721\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n722\tpub struct ValidationResult {\n723\t    /// Whether validation passed\n724\t    pub valid: bool,\n725\t    /// List of validation errors\n726\t    pub errors: Vec<ValidationError>,\n727\t    /// List of validation warnings\n728\t    pub warnings: Vec<ValidationWarning>,\n729\t}\n730\t\n731\timpl ValidationResult {\n732\t    /// Create a successful validation result\n733\t    pub fn success() -> Self {\n734\t        Self {\n735\t            valid: true,\n736\t            errors: Vec::new(),\n737\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n738\t        }\n739\t    }\n740\t\n741\t    /// Create a failed validation result\n742\t    pub fn failure(errors: Vec<ValidationError>) -> Self {\n743\t        Self {\n744\t            valid: false,\n745\t            errors,\n746\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n747\t        }\n748\t    }\n749\t\n750\t    /// Check if validation passed\n751\t    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {\n752\t        self.valid && self.errors.is_empty()\n753\t    }\n754\t\n755\t    /// Check if there are any errors\n756\t    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool {\n757\t        !self.errors.is_empty()\n758\t    }\n759\t\n760\t    /// Check if there are any warnings\n761\t    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n762\t        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n763\t    }\n764\t}\n765\t\n766\timpl Default for ValidationResult {\n767\t    fn default() -> Self {\n768\t        Self::success()\n769\t    }\n770\t}\n771\t\n772\t/// A validation error\n773\t///\n774\t/// Enhanced with error codes from bf-68hqo for comprehensive error categorization.\n775\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n776\tpub struct ValidationError {\n777\t    /// Path to the invalid element (e.g., \"server.port\")\n778\t    pub path: String,\n779\t    /// Error message\n780\t    pub message: String,\n781\t    /// Machine-readable error code from bf-68hqo hierarchy\n782\t    pub code: ErrorCode,\n783\t    /// Line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n784\t    pub line: Option<usize>,\n785\t    /// Column number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n786\t    pub column: Option<usize>,\n787\t    /// Indentation error type (if this is an indentation error)\n788\t    pub indentation_error_type: Option<crate::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::IndentationErrorType>,\n789\t    /// Delimiter error type (if this is a delimiter error)\n790\t    pub delimiter_error_type: Option<crate::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::DelimiterErrorType>,\n791\t}\n792\t\n793\timpl ValidationError {\n794\t    /// Create a new validation error\n795\t    pub fn new(path: impl Into<String>, }\n974\t}\n975\t\n976\timpl std::fmt::Display for ParseWarning {\n977\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n978\t        let location = self.line.map_or_else(|| \"<unknown>\".to_string(), |l| l.to_string());\n979\t        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", location, self.kind)\n980\t    }\n981\t}\n982\t\n983\t/// The kind of parse warning\n984\t#[derive(Debug, },\n995\t\n996\t    /// An unknown key was encountered (in lenient mode)\n997\t    ///\n998\t    /// Indicates a key that was not recognized but was not rejected\n999\t    /// due to lenient parsing mode\n1000\t    UnknownKey(String),\n1001\t\n1002\t    /// A duplicate key was encountered (in lenient mode)\n1003\t    ///\n1004\t    /// Indicates a duplicate key that was handled by taking one of         write!(f, \"warning: unknown key '{}'\", key)\n1020\t            }\n1021\t            Self::DuplicateKey(key) => {\n1022\t                write!(f, \"warning: duplicate key '{}'\",
